Initially, Murger signed his name as "Henry Mürger" to appear more elegant, but he later published his best-known works as "Henry Murger".

Nguyễn Tiến Trung (born 1983 in Hung Ha district, Thai Binh province) is a pro-democracy activist in Vietnam. As the founder and leader of the Assembly of Vietnamese Youth for Democracy Trung has been one of the outspoken political dissidents in Vietnam. He was arrested on July 7, 2009, by the public security of Vietnam for allegedly "plotting to overthrow the government of Vietnam." The accusation was persistently rejected domestically and internationally by some Vietnam analysts such as Pham Hong Son and Carl Thayer.

Howard Ehmke (April 24, 1894 – March 17, 1959) was an American right-handed pitcher in Major League Baseball from 1915 to 1930. He played for the Buffalo Blues, Detroit Tigers, Boston Red Sox, and Philadelphia Athletics.

Ehmke is best known for his 1923 no-hitter against the Athletics while with the Red Sox, and for his unexpected Game 1 start in the 1929 World Series for the Athletics, in which he struck out a then-record 13 batters. He won a World Series title that year.

Prior to playing in the major leagues, Ehmke played in the Pacific Coast League for the Los Angeles Angels and in the New York State League for the Syracuse Stars. After his baseball career, Ehmke founded the Howard Ehmke Company, manufacturing tarpaulins, tents, flags, and banners. The company is still in operation today as Ehmke Manufacturing Company.

Nathan's Famous is a fast food company founded in 1916 in Coney Island, New York City. It was started by Nathan Handwerker and his wife, Ida, who created the original hot dog recipe. The company is known for its hot dogs and the annual Nathan's Hot Dog Eating Contest held on Coney Island.

Fear (commonly stylized as fear) is the third studio album by American alternative rock band Toad the Wet Sprocket. The album is their second album for Columbia Records, and was released on August 27, 1991. It became the first commercially successful album for the band, selling over a million copies and was certified platinum three years after release, on September 1, 1994. The album reached #49 on Billboard's Top 200 Albums in September 1992. Two of the album's singles charted in the US top 40, "All I Want" and "Walk on the Ocean" which peaked at #15 and #18 on the Billboard Hot 100, respectively.

*Police Academy* is a 1984 American comedy film directed by Hugh Wilson and starring Steve Guttenberg, Kim Cattrall, and Bubba Smith. The film revolves around a new policy requiring the police academy to accept all recruits, leading to a diverse group of individuals joining the force.

The film was a box office success, grossing $149.8 million worldwide against a budget of $4.5 million, becoming the 6th highest grossing American film of 1984. Despite mixed reviews, it spawned a successful franchise of sequels, television series, and animated series.
St. Dominic's Abbey is a ruined Dominican monastery founded in 1243 in Cashel, County Tipperary, Ireland.
Jean-Philippe Mateta (born 28 June 1997) is a French professional footballer who plays as a striker for Crystal Palace. He has also represented France at the U19 and U21 levels.
Dar Hammuda Pasha is an old palace in the medina of Tunis. It is considered one of the oldest and biggest palaces of the medina that kept their original architecture.

Spindizzy is an isometric computer game released for several 8-bit home computer formats in 1986 by Electric Dreams Software. It combines action and puzzle game elements. Players must navigate a series of screens to explore a landscape suspended in a three-dimensional space. Development was headed by Paul Shirley, who drew inspiration from Ultimate Play the Game games that feature an isometric projection.

The game was successful in the United Kingdom and was well received by the video game press. Reviewers praised its visuals and design, but criticized its audio. Similarities were drawn to Marble Madness, which was released in arcades two years earlier. Spindizzy was followed by a 1990s sequel titled Spindizzy Worlds.

"Maximum Carnage" is a 14-part comic book crossover published in Marvel Comics'Spider-Man family of titles in 1993. It featured Spider-Man, Venom, and a host of other superheroes teaming up to face Venom's murderous offspring Carnage and his team of supervillains.

The 2004 New Zealand rugby league season included domestic and international competitions. The New Zealand national rugby league team drew with Australia in the Tri-Nations tournament and lost the ANZAC Test. New Zealand also hosted the 2004 Pacific Cup. The New Zealand Warriors finished 14th in the National Rugby League competition. The Mt Albert Lions won Auckland's Fox Memorial trophy, and Riccarton won the Canterbury Rugby League title.

The Lagoa do Parado Municipal Nature Park (Portuguese: *Parque Municipal Lagoa do Parado*) is in Guaratuba, Paraná. It contains diverse flora and fauna and is an important breeding ground for marine life.

George Stiebel CMG (1821 – 1896) was a Jamaican businessman and entrepreneur. He made his fortune in sea transport and arms trade, and later through a gold mine. He is known for building Devon House in Kingston, Jamaica.

Sainte-Geneviève-de-Batiscan is a parish municipality in the Les Chenaux Regional County Municipality in the Mauricie region of Quebec, Canada. The main village lies on the northeast shore of the Batiscan River. The municipality's motto is "Venus pour demeurer" ("Came to Stay"). The civil records of the Catholic parish start in 1727, with the parish canonically erected in 1833. From 1855 to 1982, the city was the seat of Champlain County. In 2021, the population was 1,028.

*Oreophrynella quelchii*, also known as the Roraima black frog, is a species of toad in the family Bufonidae. It is found on the summit of Mount Roraima in Venezuela, Guyana, and Brazil. The species' natural habitat is high montane tepui environments, where it is commonly found on open rock surfaces. The International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN) has assessed it as vulnerable due to its restricted range.

Xbase++ is an object oriented programming language which has multiple inheritance and polymorphism. It is based on the XBase language dialect and conventions. It is 100% Clipper compatible language supporting multiple inheritance, polymorphism, object oriented programming. It supports the xBase data types, including Codeblocks. With Xbase++ it is possible to generate applications for Windows NT, 95, 98, Me, 2000, XP, VISTA and Windows 7, 8, 10.

Dar Hammuda Pasha is a palace located in Tunis, Tunisia, that was built in 1630 by Hammuda Pasha Bey. It later served as a residence for the royal family and was eventually converted into a restaurant.

The ETTU Cup is an annual European table tennis club competition organized by the European Table Tennis Union (ETTU). It was first held in 1964 and has gone through several name changes, including European Fair Cities Cup and ETTU Nancy Evans Cup.

The Silent Sejm (Polish: Sejm Niemy) was a session of the Polish Sejm held on February 1, 1717. It received its name because only the speaker and a few deputies were allowed to speak. The terms of the settlement were designed by Peter the Great after the Tarnogród Confederation, and the Sejm is viewed negatively by historians for reinforcing Commonwealth military inferiority and unofficially putting it in the position of a Russian protectorate. It is seen as a precursor to the partitions of Poland.
Chillicothe is a city in Hardeman County, Texas, United States. The population was 549 at the 2020 census.

Panhard was a French motor vehicle manufacturer that began as one of the first makers of automobiles. It was a manufacturer of light tactical and military vehicles. Its final incarnation, now owned by Renault Trucks Defense, was formed by the acquisition of Panhard by Auverland in 2005, and then by Renault in 2012. In 2018 Renault Trucks Defense, ACMAT and Panhard combined under a single brand, Arquus.

Luke Michael Haines (born 7 October 1967) is an English musician, songwriter and author. He has recorded music under various names and with various bands, including The Auteurs, Baader Meinhof and Black Box Recorder.
Chillicothe is a city in Hardeman County, in the U.S. state of Texas. The population was 707 at the 2010 census.

In topology, the Denjoy–Riesz theorem states that every compact set of totally disconnected points in the Euclidean plane can be covered by a continuous image of the unit interval, without self-intersections (a Jordan arc).

*Dau al Set* was a Catalan avant-garde magazine founded by Joan-Josep Tharrats. It was first published in September 1948 and ran until 1951. The magazine was created by the *Dau al Set* group, which included artists such as Antoni Tàpies, Joan Brossa, and Modest Cuixart. Initially influenced by Dadaism, the magazine later adopted a surrealist approach, becoming a space for innovative artists. Contributors included Jean Cocteau and Francis Picabia. The Museum of Modern Art has recognized it as one of the 20 best avant-garde magazines of the 20th century.
The Denjoy–Riesz theorem states that every compact totally disconnected subset of the plane is a subset of a Jordan arc. It is named after Arnaud Denjoy and Frigyes Riesz.
The Wills Wing U2 is an American high-wing, single-place, hang glider, designed and produced by Wills Wing of Orange, California since 2003. The aircraft is supplied complete and ready-to-fly.

Phebalium nottii, commonly known as pink phebalium, is a species of shrub that is endemic to eastern Australia. It has branchlets with silvery scales, oblong to elliptical leaves, deep pink to mauve flowers arranged in umbels of up to six, with the stamens distinctively offset to one side of the flower.
The Light Helicopter Experimental (LHX) program was a 1980s United States Army helicopter procurement project to replace the AH-1 Cobra and OH-58 Kiowa helicopters.

Advance America is a conservative Christian advocacy group founded in 1980 by Eric Miller in Indiana. It publishes voter guides, informs people about legislation, and lobbies the Indiana state legislature on issues it considers to affect the family and religious freedom.

Beatrice Wishart (born 1955 or 1956) is a Scottish Liberal Democrats politician who has been the Member of the Scottish Parliament (MSP) for Shetland since 2019. Wishart currently serves as education spokesperson for her party, and has a seat on the Scottish Parliament committees for Education and Skills, for Culture, Tourism, Europe and External Affairs, and for COVID-19. She was elected at the 2019 Shetland by-election, after the sitting Liberal Democrat MSP Tavish Scott stepped down.

Pinecone Burke Provincial Park is a provincial park in British Columbia, Canada, located in Metro Vancouver near Coquitlam. Established in 1995, the park spans 380 square kilometers and is classified as a Category II protected area by the IUCN. It is bounded by Katzie, Kwikwetlem, and Stó:lō First Nations territory.

The park's history includes logging activities in the early 20th century and later conservation efforts led by groups like the Friends of Burke Mountain, who successfully advocated for its protected status. The park's diverse geography includes mountains such as Pinecone Peak, old-growth forests, alpine lakes, and remnant ice fields.

Pinecone Burke is home to a variety of wildlife, including mammals like black-tailed deer, mountain goats, bears, and various marine species, amphibians, birds, fungi, and plants. The park also contains Widgeon Valley's nationally recognized wetlands. Recreational activities include hiking, camping, backcountry skiing, and canoeing. The park is managed by BC Parks, which is currently collaborating with the city of Coquitlam and the Katzie First Nation to develop a management plan focused on balancing recreational use, conservation, and nature-based tourism.

Jeremy Maurice Jacobs Sr. (born January 21, 1940) is an American billionaire businessman, the owner of the Boston Bruins and chairman of Delaware North. Forbes magazine ranks him as 481st richest person in the world.

The 1999 Brickyard 400, the 6th running of the event, was a NASCAR Winston Cup Series race held on August 7, 1999 at Indianapolis Motor Speedway in Speedway, Indiana. Contested at 160 laps on the 2.5 mile (4.023 km) speedway, it was the 20th race of the 1999 NASCAR Winston Cup Series season. Dale Jarrett of Robert Yates Racing won the race.

The University of Florida's (UF) online clinical toxicology distance education programs cater to working professionals, including physicians, nurses, physician assistants, first responders, and poison control center professionals. The programs focus on toxicants, drugs of abuse, drug analysis, and biotransformation, as well as the treatment of poisoned or overdosed patients. Classes are taught by internationally recognized faculty with expertise in clinical toxicology, medicine, pharmacy, and pharmacology.

Each course within UF's program is conducted online and is made up of specific topic modules. Most modules contain course notes supplemented with images, animations, and case studies. Students are provided constant access to course modules, which are released over the duration of the UF semester.
Air Vice Marshal Christina Reid Elliot, CB, CBE, DL is a retired senior Royal Air Force officer.

Password synchronization is a process, usually supported by software such as password managers, through which a user maintains a single password across multiple IT systems.

Provided that all the systems enforce mutually-compatible password standards (e.g. concerning minimum and maximum password length, supported characters, etc.), the user can choose a new password at any time and deploy the same password on his or her own login accounts across multiple, linked systems.

Where different systems have mutually incompatible standards regarding what can be stored in a password field, the user may be forced to choose more than one (but still fewer than the number of systems) passwords. This may happen, for example, where the maximum password length on one system is shorter than the minimum length in another, or where one system requires use of a punctuation mark but another forbids it.

Password synchronization is a function of certain identity management systems and it is considered easier to implement than enterprise single sign-on (SSO), as there is normally no client software deployment or need for active user enrollment.

Matthew "Matt" Johnson (born August 15, 1961) is an English singer, songwriter, and musician. He is best known as the frontman of the band The The, which he formed in 1979. In 2017, The The released their first single in 15 years, and began a comeback tour in 2018.

Alan Arnett McLeod VC (1899–1918) was a Canadian pilot during World War I. He was awarded the Victoria Cross at age 18 for his actions on March 27, 1918, when he and his observer were attacked by eight German aircraft. McLeod was wounded but managed to land his burning plane in No Man's Land, saving his observer. He died of Spanish influenza shortly after returning to Canada.

Hadia Tajik (born 18 July 1983) is a Norwegian politician from the Labour Party. She served as Minister of Culture from 2012 to 2013, becoming the youngest minister in Norway at 29 and the first Muslim in the cabinet. Tajik was Minister of Labour and Social Inclusion from 2021 to 2022. She has been a Member of Parliament representing Oslo (2009-2017) and Rogaland (2017-present). Tajik also served as Deputy Leader of the Labour Party from 2015 to 2022. She resigned as minister in March 2022.
Thaddäus Huber (8 May 1742 – 27 February 1798) was an Austrian violinist and composer.

Lygodium microphyllum (commonly known as, variously, climbing maidenhair fern, Old World climbing fern, small-leaf climbing fern, or snake fern) is a climbing fern originating in tropical Africa, Southeast Asia, Melanesia and Australia. It is an invasive weed in Florida where it invades open forest and wetland areas. The type specimen was collected in the vicinity of Nabúa, on the island of Luzon in the Philippines by Luis Née.

Hulu Selangor is a district in Selangor, Malaysia. It contains the towns of Serendah, Batang Kali and Kuala Kubu Bharu. Its principal town is Kuala Kubu Bharu. The district is located in the northeastern part of Selangor and borders the state of Perak to the north, Pahang to the east, Sabak Bernam district to the northwest, Kuala Selangor district to its southwest and Gombak district to the south. Selangor River sources from this area, hence giving the district's name.

Derek Christopher Shepherd, M.D., F.A.C.S., is a fictional neurosurgeon from the ABC medical drama *Grey's Anatomy*, portrayed by Patrick Dempsey. He first appeared in the pilot episode "A Hard Day's Night" in 2005 and remained a series regular until his death in the season 11 episode "How to Save a Life" in 2015, later reappearing in a recurring role in season 17.

Introduced as the new Head of Neurosurgery at Seattle Grace Hospital, Derek is a graduate of Bowdoin College and Columbia University. He specializes in complex brain and spine conditions, earning a reputation for taking on challenging cases. His romantic relationship with intern Meredith Grey is a central storyline, eventually leading to marriage and children. Derek's past in New York City, including his divorce from Addison Montgomery and friendship with Mark Sloan, also plays a significant role in his character arc.

Maris Harvey Taylor (June 5, 1876 – May 16, 1982), also known as M. Harvey Taylor or Harvey Taylor, was an American politician from Pennsylvania who served as a Republican member of the Pennsylvania Senate for the 15th district from 1941 to 1964 including as President pro tempore from 1945 to 1964. He was a political boss who held multiple political positions in Dauphin County and statewide.

Maris Harvey Taylor (June 5, 1876 – May 16, 1982) was an American politician from Pennsylvania. He served as a Pennsylvania State Senator for the 15th district from 1941 to 1964 and as President Pro Tempore. He was also chairman of the Republican State Committee of Pennsylvania. The M. Harvey Taylor Memorial Bridge is named in his honor.

The Parapetí River is a river in Bolivia that has its source on the eastern side the Andes, flows through the Gran Chaco of Bolivia and ends in the marshes of the Bañados de Izozog depression. The drainage basin is 25,300 km2, and with the inclusion of the Bañados of Izozog is 61,903 km2.

Jeff Danielson (born August 6, 1970) is a former Democratic Iowa State Senator. Having served the 30th Senate District between 2013 and 2019, he had also previously served the 10th Senate District up until redistricting, between 2005 and 2013.

Danielson served on following committees: Appropriations (Vice Chair), Economic Growth, State Government (Chair), Transportation, Veterans Affairs (Vice Chair), and the Administration and Regulation Appropriations Subcommittee (Vice Chair). Permanent statutory committees he serves on include: Public Retirement Systems and State Government Efficiency Review. Danielson additionally serves on the Iowa Capital Investment Board.

His prior public service experience includes experience as the Commissioner of the Iowa Department of Transportation and the Waterloo Planning, Programming, and Zoning Commission. He is a U.S. Navy veteran and serves the community of Cedar Falls as a professional firefighter.

Vibal Publishing House is a Philippine publishing company founded in 1953 by Hilarion P. Vibal and Esther Asuncion-Vibal. Headquartered in Quezon City, it produces books, textbooks, e-books, and other educational materials distributed nationwide. Vibal introduced the first full-color textbook in the Philippines in 2004. The company has also received awards from the National Book Development Board for its textbooks. Vibal has partnered with organizations like Microsoft and the Department of Education to create digital classrooms.

Michael Korovkin is an anthropologist specializing in political and economic anthropology, symbolic expression, and medical anthropology of stress. He has taught at universities in Canada and Italy and consulted for organizations including the UN. He is also the author of several novels, some published under pseudonyms, and books of poetry.

Half Man Half Biscuit are an English indie rock band, formed in 1984 in Birkenhead, Merseyside. Known for their satirical lyrics referencing British popular culture, they have been championed by DJ John Peel and praised for their unique blend of wit and social commentary.

Police Academy is a 1984 American comedy film directed by Hugh Wilson in his directorial debut, and distributed by Warner Bros. Pictures. Its story follows a new recruitment policy for an unnamed police department's academy that is required to take in any recruit who wishes to try out to be a police officer. The film stars Steve Guttenberg, Kim Cattrall, and G.W. Bailey.

The film was produced by The Ladd Company. It premiered on March 23, 1984. It grossed $8.5 million in its opening weekend and over $149 million worldwide, against a budget of $4.5 million, and remains the most successful film of the series as of 2022. The film spawned six sequels in the Police Academy franchise.

An Ziwen (Chinese: 安子文; September 25, 1909 – June 25, 1980) was a Chinese Communist revolutionary and politician. He served as Head of the Organization Department of the Chinese Communist Party from 1956 to 1966 and Deputy Secretary of the Central Commission for Discipline Inspection from 1949 to 1955. An was persecuted during the Cultural Revolution but was rehabilitated in 1978 and appointed Vice President of the Central Party School. His wife was Liu Jingxiong, and his brother was An Zhiwen.

OpenDataSoft is a software company founded in 2011 by Jean-Marc Lazard, Franck Carassus, and David Thoumas. It provides a SaaS solution for organizations to share, visualize, and organize data. OpenDataSoft is headquartered in Boston and Paris. It has received funding from firms including Aurinvest, Salesforce Ventures, and Aster Capital. The company also hosts an annual summit called "Data on Board".

In rotorcraft aerodynamics, **dissymmetry of lift** refers to the unequal lift forces experienced by the advancing and retreating blades of a helicopter rotor in forward flight. As a helicopter moves forward, the blade advancing into the oncoming airflow experiences a higher relative airspeed than the retreating blade. This difference in airspeed results in greater lift production on the advancing side of the rotor disc.

Dissymmetry of lift is countered through blade flapping and cyclic feathering. Blade flapping allows the advancing blade to rise and decrease its angle of attack, while the retreating blade falls and increases its angle of attack. Cyclic feathering involves continuously adjusting the pitch angle of each blade as it rotates. Failure to compensate for dissymmetry of lift can lead to retreating blade stall, which can severely limit the maximum forward speed of the helicopter. In helicopters with coaxial rotors, the effects of dissymmetry of lift are cancelled out.
Babócsa (Lang-de, Lang-hr) is a village in Somogy County, Hungary.

David William Heneker (1906–2001) was an English composer and lyricist. After a military career, he became a songwriter, and later a musical theatre composer, known for *Half a Sixpence* (1963) and *Charlie Girl* (1965).

Calamus is a genus of flowering plants in the palm family Arecaceae that are among several genera known as rattan palms. There are an estimated 400 species in this genus, all native to tropical and subtropical Asia, Africa, and Australia.
The *Oberkommando der Luftwaffe* (OKL) was the high command of the Nazi German *Luftwaffe* (air force), established in 1935 and disbanded in 1945.

Ja Lama (Lang-mn, also known as Dambiijantsan, Lang-mn or Dambiijaa, Lang-mn; 1862–1922) was an adventurer and warlord of unknown birth and background who fought successive campaigns against the rule of the Qing dynasty in western Mongolia between 1890 and 1922. He claimed to be a Buddhist lama, though it is not clear whether he actually was one, as well as a grandson and later the reincarnation of Amursana, the Khoid-Oirat prince who led the last great Mongol uprising against the Qing in 1757. He was one of the commanders of Mongolian forces that liberated Khovd city from Qing control in 1912.

Mild androgen insensitivity syndrome (MAIS) is the mildest form of androgen insensitivity syndrome (AIS), a genetic condition that results in impaired androgen receptor function. Individuals with MAIS are born phenotypically male with fully masculinized genitalia, but may experience impaired virilization or spermatogenesis. MAIS is often diagnosed during male infertility evaluations, with an estimated 2-3% of infertile men having androgen receptor gene mutations, and MAIS potentially responsible for 40% of male infertility.

Phenotypes of MAIS can include isolated infertility, mild gynecomastia, decreased secondary terminal hair, high-pitched voice, or minor hypospadias repair in childhood. Spinal and bulbar muscular atrophy (SBMA), also known as Kennedy's disease, is technically a variant of MAIS, but presents with neuromuscular defects and signs of androgen inaction in adulthood. Diagnosis of MAIS is challenging due to its mild presentation, often requiring confirmation of an androgen receptor gene mutation. Management is limited to symptomatic treatment, such as surgical correction of gynecomastia, hypospadias repair, and testosterone supplementation.

Mansfield is a borough in Tioga County, Pennsylvania, United States. Settled in 1804 and incorporated in 1857, it is the home of Mansfield University of Pennsylvania. The population was 2,839 at the 2020 census.

Henry Nicholas John Gunther (1895–1918) was an American soldier who is generally recognized as the last soldier killed in action during World War I. He was killed on November 11, 1918, at 10:59 a.m., one minute before the armistice took effect. He was posthumously restored to the rank of sergeant and awarded the Distinguished Service Cross.
**S. P. Singh** (born 1948) is an Indian biochemist and professor.
In mathematics, Casey's theorem, also known as the generalized Ptolemy's theorem, is a theorem in Euclidean geometry named after the Irish mathematician John Casey.

The Forging of a Rebel is an autobiographical trilogy of books written by the Spanish author, broadcaster and journalist Arturo Barea. They were first published during the author's exile in the United Kingdom after the Spanish Civil War.

Ultrasound-modulated optical tomography (UOT) is a form of tomography involving ultrasound. It is used in imaging of biological soft tissues and has potential applications for early cancer detection. Like optical techniques, this method provides high contrast, and the use of ultrasound also provides high resolution.

The 2014 Vodacom Cup was contested between sixteen teams from South Africa and Kenya from 7 March to 16 May 2014. The teams were geographically divided into two sections, with the top four teams in each section qualifying for the title play-offs. Griquas won the competition, their fifth title, after beating the Golden Lions in the final.

Salah Shahrour (born February 1, 1988) is a Syrian footballer who plays as a defender for Al-Nejmeh. He began his senior career with Al-Ittihad, helping them win the 2010 AFC Cup. Shahrour has been a member of the Syria national team since 2006 and played in the 2005 FIFA U-20 World Cup.

Pivotal (1993–2021) was a British Thoroughbred racehorse and sire. Bred and owned by Cheveley Park Stud, he was trained by Sir Mark Prescott and ridden by George Duffield. In 1996, he won the King's Stand Stakes and the Nunthorpe Stakes. Pivotal was the first foal sired by Polar Falcon. After retiring from racing, Pivotal became a successful breeding stallion. He was retired from stud duties in February 2021 and died in November of the same year.

Sir Robin Buchanan Nicholson FRS FREng (born 1934) is a British metallurgist. He studied at the University of Cambridge, earning a PhD in metallurgy. Nicholson was Chief Scientific Adviser to the Cabinet Office from 1983 to 1985. He was also a director of Pilkington, Rolls-Royce, and BP.
The National Conference of State Legislatures (NCSL) is a non-governmental organization founded in 1975. It has offices in Washington, D.C., and Denver, Colorado.
Pelican Waters is a coastal suburb of Caloundra, Queensland, Australia. It had a population of 6,277 in the 2016 census. It was established in 1995.

"Maximum Carnage" is a 14-part superhero comic book crossover published in 1993 by Marvel Comics. The story features Spider-Man and Venom teaming up to battle Carnage and his allies who are on a killing spree in New York City.

Ultrasound-modulated optical tomography (UOT) is an imaging technique for soft tissue. It combines ultrasound and optics, using ultrasound to modulate light within a medium. By analyzing the reemitted light, UOT can create images of the medium's interior. It was first proposed in 2013 for virus detection.

*Speshow* is Show Lo's fourth studio album, released on November 17, 2006, by Avex Taiwan. The album was a commercial success in Taiwan, debuting at number one on the G-Music Weekly Top 20 Mandarin and Combo Charts, and the Five Music Chart. It charted continuously in the Mandarin Chart for 27 weeks.

Two songs from *Speshow*, "好朋友" (Good Friends) and "精舞門" (Dance Gate), were listed on Hit Fm Taiwan's Hit Fm Annual Top 100 Singles Chart for 2006. "精舞門" also won a Top 10 Songs of the Year award at the 2007 HITO Radio Music Awards. Four versions of the album were released.

The Ministry of War of the Kingdom of Bavaria was the successor to the Hofkriegsrat (court war council) founded in 1620. It was renamed several times, eventually becoming the Kriegsministerium in 1825. In 1918, it was replaced by the Ministry of Military Affairs, which was then disbanded in 1919 and succeeded by the Reichswehrbefehlsstelle Bayern.

The 1912–13 season was Blackpool F.C.'s 16th season (13th consecutive) in the Football League. They competed in the twenty-team Division Two, then the second tier of English football, finishing bottom. Their application for re-election was successful.

George Wilson was the club's top scorer, with ten goals.

*Phebalium nottii*, commonly known as the **pink phebalium**, is a shrub in the family Rutaceae and is endemic to inland Queensland and New South Wales. It typically grows to 1–3 m (3 ft 3 in – 9 ft 10 in) high and has pink to deep mauve flowers that occur in spring.

JD Eicher (born in Youngstown, Ohio) is an American alt-pop/indie singer-songwriter, producer, and novelist. Active since 2006, he plays vocals, guitar, and keys. Eicher began playing guitar at age 12 and debuted his first song while attending Canfield High School. He earned a Bachelor of Science degree in music and business from Westminster College.

While at Westminster College, Eicher formed JD Eicher & the Goodnights. The group released a trilogy of albums: *The Shape of Things* (2009), *Shifting* (2011), and *Into Place* (2013). In 2016, Eicher provided an EP soundtrack to Nicholas Sparks' novel *Two by Two*. In 2022, he released his first album under AntiFragile Music, *Majesto Sessions*, in accompaniment to his first novel, *The Lights Along Majesto*. Eicher is also the creator of JD's Summer Songfest, an annual event in Youngstown, Ohio, that benefits local charities.
Thaddäus Huber (born in Niederhollabrunn, Lower Austria; died 1798 in Vienna) was an Austrian violinist and composer.

Phussa Buddha is one of the 29 Buddhas mentioned in the Buddhavamsa. He is venerated by Theravada, Mahayana, and Vajrayana Buddhists and is said to have lived 90,000 years. According to Buddhist tradition, Phussa was born in Kāsika to King Jayasena and Queen Sirimā. He renounced his royal life after the birth of his son and attained enlightenment under the Maha Bodhi tree. He was succeeded by Vipaśyin Buddha.
Giorgio Pisanò (Ferrara, 30 January 1924 – Milan, 17 October 1997) was an Italian journalist, essayist and fascist politician.
Luise Koch (1860–1934) was a German politician, suffragist, and educator. She founded the Bremen chapter of the German Association for Women's Suffrage.

Ryan Higa (born June 6, 1990), also known as nigahiga (/ˈniːɡəhiːɡə/ NEE-ɡə-HEE-ɡə), is an American Internet personality. Best known for his comedy videos on YouTube, Higa began making YouTube videos in 2006 and was one of the most popular creators on the platform in its early years. His main YouTube channel, nigahiga, was the most subscribed channel on YouTube for 677 consecutive days from 2009 to 2011, an achievement that has only been surpassed by PewDiePie and T-Series. Higa was the first person to reach the milestones of 2 million and 3 million subscribers on YouTube. Higa launched a podcast in 2018 called Off the Pill, which has featured YouTubers and celebrities such as KevJumba, Andrew Yang, and Jeremy Lin. In 2020, Higa started streaming on Twitch, where he reacts to his past videos and broadcasts video game content, most notably in Valorant.

As of November 2022, he has over 21 million subscribers on YouTube and 756,000 followers on Twitch. Higa has won a Shorty Award, has been named Forbes Top 30 Under 30, and has been nominated for three more Shorty Awards, six Streamy Awards, and five Teen Choice Awards.

Outside of his content on YouTube and Twitch, he has also published a memoir Ryan Higa's How to Write Good and appeared in feature films Tell Me How I Die (2016) and Finding'Ohana (2021).
The Light Helicopter Experimental (LHX) program was a 1980s U.S. Army initiative to replace several existing helicopter types.

Sainte-Geneviève-de-Batiscan is a parish municipality in the Mauricie region of the province of Quebec in Canada. This municipality is part of the Les Chenaux Regional County Municipality in Batiscanie and in the administrative region of Mauricie. It is located along the Batiscan River.

The town of Sainte-Geneviève-de-Batiscan was the seat of the Lordship of Batiscan from 1665 and Champlain County from 1855 to 1981. During the first centuries of its history, the economy of the area has been focused on forestry, agriculture, fishing and river transport. Sainte-Geneviève was also a prominent center of trade for the Batiscanie.

Azerbaijan—France relations are the bilateral relations between the Republic of Azerbaijan and the French Republic in the political, socio-economic, cultural, and other spheres.

Cooperation is carried out in such areas as energy (Total), environment (Suez CNIM), transport (Alstom, Thales, Iveco, SYSTRA), agriculture (Lactalis, Danone), space industry (Airbus, Thales), gas sector, security, culture, hotel business (Accor), banking (Societe Generale), aviation, etc.

SMS Eber was an Iltis-class gunboat built for the German Navy in 1902–1903. She was laid down at AG Vulcan in Stettin, launched on 6 June 1903, and commissioned on 15 September 1903. Eber was armed with two 10.5 cm guns. After Brazil entered World War I, the ship was scuttled by her crew in 1917 to prevent its capture.
Timothy John Greene (born 1969 in Cape Town, South Africa) is a South African actor, film director, and writer. He is also known as Tim Greene and cited under that name in most articles about him.
The Mali–Netherlands relations refers to the current diplomatic and military ties between the Republic of Mali and the Kingdom of the Netherlands.

The Great Sandy Desert (GSD) is an interim Australian bioregion, located in the northeast of Western Australia straddling the Pilbara and southern Kimberley regions and extending east into the Northern Territory. It is the second largest desert in Australia after the Great Victoria Desert and encompasses an area of 284,993 square kilometres (110,036 sq mi). The Gibson Desert lies to the south and the Tanami Desert lies to the east of the Great Sandy Desert.

*The Forging of a Rebel* is a trilogy of autobiographical novels by Spanish author Arturo Barea, written in Spanish and translated into English by his wife, Ilsa Pollak. The novels cover Barea's life from his childhood in Madrid to his exile in the United Kingdom.
S. P. Singh (born 1 February 1948), is a professor of biochemistry.

The President's Scholarship is an undergraduate scholarship awarded in Singapore. It was inaugurated in 1966, after Singapore's independence, but its roots trace back to the Queen's Scholarship, which was founded in 1885 by Cecil Clementi Smith. The Queen's Scholarship was replaced by the Singapore State Scholarship in 1959, and then by the Yang di-Pertuan Negara Scholarship in 1964. Many recipients of the President's Scholarship have gone on to hold high-ranking positions in the civil service.
Felix Johannes Oinas (6 March 1911 – 25 September 2004) was an Estonian folklorist, linguist, and translator.

DecembeRadio is the eponymous major label debut album by the band DecembeRadio. Produced by Scotty Wilbanks, the album features a guitar-driven sound that quickly earned it comparisons to The Black Crowes, King's X, Aerosmith and Free. The album was nominated for a "Best Rock or Rap Gospel Album" Grammy Award, and won the 2007 Dove Award for "Rock Album of the Year". Two of the album's tracks were also nominated for Dove Awards: "Drifter" for "Song of the Year", and "Dangerous" for "Rock Recorded Song of the Year".

Rami Mehmed Pasha (1645 – 1706) was an Ottoman statesman and man of letters. He served as Grand Vizier from January to August 1703, and as Ottoman Governor of Egypt from 1704 to 1706. He also represented the Ottoman Empire in the Treaty of Karlowitz.

HNoMS *Rauma* was a Royal Norwegian Navy Otra-class minesweeper built in 1939. In April 1940, it fought invading German forces in the Battle of Horten Harbor, damaging a warship. Seized by the Germans, it served as *Kamerun*. Recommissioned by Norway in 1947, it served until 1959 and was scrapped in 1963.
Roy Noble OBE, DL, K.St.J (born 1942) is a Welsh educator, broadcaster, and writer, known for his work on BBC Radio Wales.

Azerbaijan–France relations formally began on February 21, 1992, after France recognized Azerbaijan's independence in January 1992. Fifty-three documents constitute the legal framework of the bilateral ties. Cultural exchange is promoted through events like "Days of Azerbaijani Culture" in France. The French-Azerbaijani University (UFAZ) was established in 2014.

*DecembeRadio* is the eponymous second studio album by the American Christian rock band DecembeRadio, released on June 27, 2006, through Slanted Records. It was recorded at Southern Tracks Recording in Atlanta, Georgia, between January 26 and February 1, 2006, and produced by Scotty Wilbanks. The album won a Dove Award for Rock Album of the Year in 2007. An expanded edition of the album was released on November 13, 2007, featuring a new single and an acoustic version of "Love Found Me (Love's Got a Hold)".

ESET NOD32 Antivirus, commonly known as NOD32, is an antivirus software package made by the Slovak company ESET. ESET NOD32 Antivirus is sold in two editions, Home Edition and Business Edition. The Business Edition packages add ESET Remote Administrator allowing for server deployment and management, mirroring of threat signature database updates and the ability to install on Microsoft Windows Server operating systems.

Andre Tierre Royal (born December 1, 1972) is a former professional American football player in the National Football League who played linebacker for five seasons for the Carolina Panthers and the Indianapolis Colts. Royal had 140 career tackles, 6 solo sacks, 4 forced fumbles and 2 fumble recoveries. Royal was traded to the Indianapolis Colts following several off-the-field incidents prior to the start of the season with The New Orleans Saints.

Dulce Enemiga (English title:Sweet Enemy) is a Venezuelan telenovela written by Valentina Párraga and produced by Venevisión in 1995. This telenovela lasted 153 episodes and was distributed internationally by Venevisión International.

Gigi Zanchetta and Guillermo Dávila starred as the protagonists with Natalia Streignard as the antagonist.

Natalia Maliszewska (born 16 September 1995) is a Polish short track speed skater. She won silver medal at the 2018 World Short Track Speed Skating Championships and gold medal at the 2019 European Short Track Speed Skating Championships.

Pinecone Burke Provincial Park is a provincial park in British Columbia, Canada. It extends from the southwest corner of Garibaldi Provincial Park, west of Pitt Lake and Pitt River to include Burke Mountain in the City of Coquitlam. Most of the park is unserviced wilderness with very rough trails such as the Fools Gold Trail in the Boise Valley. There is canoe access to Widgeon Slough and from there hiking trails that lead to Widgeon Lake, and a network of old logging roads, hiking and mountain biking trails on Burke Mountain. The parks boundaries were established in 1995 by the provincial government and covers 38,000 hectares. Parts of the parks boundaries are located in the Katzie First Nation, Kwikwetlem First Nation, and Stó:lō First Nations territories. The parks diverse ecosystems supports several different species on land and in its inlet. There are 6 species that have been labeled as sensitive or vulnerable that live in the park. Before the parks protected status the area was logged and mined. It also was used for recreational purposes such as hiking and rock climbing. Visitors can use the park for hiking, camping, back country skiing and snowshoeing.

The French brig *Sphinx* was a *Sylphe*-class brig built in Genoa in 1813. The British captured her in 1814 and commissioned her as HMS *Sphynx*, renaming her HMS *Regent* circa 1815. The Royal Navy apparently transferred her to HM Customs as a revenue cruiser, and sold her in 1824. She then became the Colombian brig *Victoria*. Her subsequent fate is unknown.

The Ladies of the House, also known under the working title of Stripped, is a 2014 grindhouse film and the directorial debut of actor John Stuart Wildman. The film had its world premiere on April 4, 2014 at the Dallas International Film Festival, and marks the first non-pornographic film by the former adult actress Belladonna.

Funding for The Ladies of the House was partially raised through a successful Kickstarter campaign.

Sylvia Fine (August 29, 1913 – October 28, 1991) was an American composer, lyricist, and producer. She is best known as the wife of Danny Kaye, whose career she managed and for whom she wrote many songs. Fine also worked in television, earning a Peabody Award for her series *Musical Comedy Tonight*.

Desmond "Dessie" Glynn (7 June 1928 – 6 January 2017) was an Irish footballer who played as a forward. At youth level, he won the FAI Youth Cup with Johnville in 1946.

Glynn is best known for his time at Drumcondra, where he won the League of Ireland title in 1949 and the FAI Cup in 1954. He was Drumcondra's top scorer for six seasons, and the League of Ireland's top scorer in 1951. He also played for Shelbourne.

Glynn was capped twice for the Republic of Ireland, scoring the winning goal on his debut against West Germany in 1951. He also represented the League of Ireland XI.
Coventry RLFC was a semi-professional rugby league team based in Coventry, West Midlands, England that played in the Northern Rugby Football Union League in the 1910–11, 1911–12, and 1912–13 seasons.

Beatrice Wishart (born 1955 or 1956) is a Scottish Liberal Democrat politician serving as the Member of the Scottish Parliament (MSP) for Shetland since 2019. Prior to her election to Holyrood, she was a councilor for the Lerwick South ward on the Shetland Islands Council, where she also served as depute convenor. Wishart was first elected to the Scottish Parliament in a 2019 by-election and was re-elected in 2021.
Joseph Biddle Wilkinson Jr. (1845–1915) was a member of the Board of General Appraisers. He was also a Confederate officer and Louisiana planter.

The 2004 New Zealand rugby league season was the 97th season of rugby league that had been played in New Zealand. The main feature of the year was the fifth season of the Bartercard Cup competition that was run by the New Zealand Rugby League. The Mt Albert Lions won the Cup by defeating the Marist Richmond Brothers 40–20 in the Grand Final.

Woo In-hee (c. late 1930s or early 1940s – 1981) was a North Korean actress, known as the "most beautiful woman in North Korea". She achieved stardom in *The Tale of Chunhyang* and other films, earning the title of People's Actress. After an affair with Kim Jong-il, she had a relationship with a Zainichi Korean man. Following his accidental death, Woo mentioned Kim during interrogation and was subsequently executed by firing squad in front of 6,000 people.
The Fine Arts Department (Lang-th, RTGS: Krom Sinlapakon) is a government department of Thailand, under the Ministry of Culture. Its mission is managing the country's cultural heritage.

A diatreme is a volcanic pipe formed by a gaseous explosion that originates from a deep source. These formations are associated with the presence of groundwater during igneous intrusion. Maar-diatreme volcanoes are relatively common, ranking as the second most frequent type of volcano on continents and islands. Examples include the Blackfoot and Cross diatremes in British Columbia, Canada.

Diatremes can have economic significance when associated with mineral deposits. Notably, kimberlite magma, originating from the upper mantle, can form diatremes. Kimberlite intrusions may carry diamonds from depths of 150-200 kilometers, making these diatremes potential sources of diamonds as xenoliths.
Jamil Baroody (1905 – March 4, 1979) was a Saudi diplomat who served as a representative to the United Nations from the organization's first meeting in 1946 until his death.

The 2021 Little League World Series was held from August 19 to August 29, 2021. Due to the COVID-19 pandemic, only 16 teams from the United States competed. The tournament field consisted of the top two teams from eight regional tournaments. The teams were divided into the Tom Seaver and Hank Aaron championship brackets.

Taylor North Little League from Taylor, Michigan, won the tournament, defeating West Side Little League from Hamilton, Ohio, in the championship game. The championship game was a rematch of the Great Lakes regional final. Ella Bruning of the team from Abilene, Texas, was one of the few girls to compete in the series.

The MLB Little League Classic game was played on August 22 between the Cleveland Indians and Los Angeles Angels, with Cleveland winning 3–0.

Sussex Archaeological Collections is an annual peer-reviewed academic journal covering archaeological topics. The journal is published by the Sussex Archaeological Society and was established in 1848.

Al-Azhar Park (Lang-ar) is a public park located in Cairo, Egypt.

Among several honors, this park is listed as one of the world's sixty great public spaces by the Project for Public Spaces. The park was created by the Historic Cities Support Programme of the Aga Khan Trust for Culture, an entity of the Aga Khan Development Network. The park was developed at a cost in excess of USD $30 million, its funding a gift to Cairo from Aga Khan IV, a descendant of the Fatimid Caliphs of Cairo.

The park project, an urbanism initiative, included:

Powers Auditorium, in Youngstown, Ohio is one of the largest auditoriums in the Youngstown-Warren area. The facility is the main venue of downtown Youngstown's DeYor Performing Arts Center. The complex also includes the Adler Art Academy, Beecher Flad Pavilion, and Ford Family Recital Hall. Originally built in 1931 as the Warner Theatre, the former movie palace was renovated and reopened as Powers Auditorium in 1969.

The main tenant of Powers Auditorium is the Youngstown Symphony, which performs from October through May. The facility also hosts other musical acts, touring Broadway productions, and locally produced theater (primarily from Ballet Western Reserve and Easy Street Productions, two Youngstown-based theater companies).

Mam Tor is a 517 m (1,696 ft) hill near Castleton in the High Peak of Derbyshire, England. Its name means "mother hill", so called because frequent landslips on its eastern face have resulted in a multitude of "mini-hills" beneath it. These landslips, which are caused by unstable lower layers of shale, also give the hill its alternative name of Shivering Mountain.

In 1979, the continual battle to maintain the A625 road (Sheffield to Chapel en le Frith) on the crumbling eastern side of the hill was lost when the road officially closed as a through-route, with the Fox House to Castleton section of the road being re-designated as the A6187.

The hill is crowned by a late Bronze Age and early Iron Age univallate hill fort, and two Bronze Age bowl barrows. At the base of the Tor and nearby are four show caves: Blue John Cavern, Speedwell Cavern, Peak Cavern and Treak Cliff Cavern where lead, Blue John, fluorspar and other minerals were once mined. Mam Tor was declared to be one of the Seven Wonders of the Peak by Thomas Hobbes in his 1636 book De Mirabilibus Pecci.

Simon Jenkins rates the panorama from Kinder Scout to Stanage Edge as one of the top ten in England.

The Amritsar–Jamnagar Expressway is a 1,257 km long, four-lane expressway in India, connecting Amritsar, Punjab to Jamnagar, Gujarat. It passes through the states of Punjab, Haryana, Rajasthan, and Gujarat. The expressway is part of the Bharatmala Pariyojana Phase-I, funded by the National Investment and Infrastructure Fund (NIIF). It aims to connect three oil refineries at Bathinda, Barmer, and Jamnagar.

Construction was divided into 8 sections comprising 30 packages, with an expected completion date of September 2023. The route begins in Kapurthala district, Punjab, and traverses through Haryana, Rajasthan, and Gujarat, connecting major cities like Bathinda, Bikaner, Jodhpur and Barmer. In Rajasthan, nearly 50% of the expressway's length lies. The project, including land acquisition, is valued at approximately ₹80,000 crores. Existing highways will be upgraded to provide connectivity to other ports and cities.

*A Beautifully Foolish Endeavor* is a 2020 speculative fiction novel by Hank Green. It follows April May's friends as they grapple with her death and the changed world. They confront Altus, a company manipulating humanity through a virtual reality simulation, and work to shut it down.

In mathematics, the dimension of a vector space is the number of vectors in any basis for the space. This number is always the same for any basis of a given space. As an example, the vector space \\( \\mathbb{R}^n \\) has \\( n \\) as a standard basis, and therefore \\( n \\). The dimension of a subspace is less than or equal to the dimension of its superspace.

Any two finite dimensional vector spaces over a field \\( F \\) with the same dimension are isomorphic. The dimension can also be characterized as the trace of the identity operator, which allows for generalizations to situations where there is a trace but no natural basis.
The Lagoa do Parado Municipal Nature Park (Lang-pt) is a municipal nature park in the state of Paraná, Brazil. It protects an area of marshland rich in biodiversity.

Jasaw Chan Kʼawiil I (reigned 682-734) was an Ajaw of the Maya city-state of Tikal. His defeat of Calakmul in 695 marked a resurgence in Tikal's power. He was buried in Tikal Temple I, while his wife, Lady Lahan Unen Moʼ, was buried in Tikal Temple II.

Lala Hsu (Chinese: 徐佳瑩; born December 20, 1984) is a Taiwanese singer-songwriter. She gained prominence after winning the third season of the One Million Star singing competition in 2008. Prior to her debut as a singer, Hsu worked as a nurse.

In 2009, Hsu released her debut album, *Lala Hsu*, which earned her six nominations at the 21st Golden Melody Awards, including Best New Artist, which she won. She has since released six studio albums, including *Limits* (2010), *Ideal Life* (2012), *Missing* (2014), *The Inner Me* (2017), and *Gei* (2022). Hsu's album *The Inner Me* won Best Mandarin Album and Best Mandarin Female Singer at the 29th Golden Melody Awards.

Hsu is known for her unique musical style, which blends Mandopop with elements of Taiwanese opera. She often incorporates both Mandarin and Hokkien languages into her lyrics. She participated in *I Am a Singer* in 2016 and *Singer* in 2020. Hsu married director Bill Chia in 2018, and they have one child.

The Wills Wing U2 is an American recreational glider introduced as an intermediate-level glider with near competition-level performance. It is made from aluminum alloy tubing and Dacron sailcloth. It is also sold as the Techma Sport U2.

Victory Day over Nazism in World War II (Lang-uk) or Victory Day (Lang-uk) is a national holiday and a day off in Ukraine. It was first celebrated on 9 May 2015 and follows the Day of Remembrance and Reconciliation on May 8 (which pays tribute to the victims of World War II and was also first celebrated in 2015). The holiday replaced the Soviet Union/Russian Federation "Victory Day", which was celebrated in the post-Soviet Union states, including Ukraine, until 2014 inclusive.

Oreal Donald Perras (August 25, 1942 – February 18, 2017) was a Canadian professional wrestler, better known by the ring name "The Russian Bear" Ivan Koloff. He was the third wrestler to hold the WWWF Championship.

Mélite, or The False Letters, is a comedy in five acts by Pierre Corneille. Written in 1625, it is Corneille's first play and debuted on stage in December 1629 in Berthaud’s Jeu de paume court, and was performed by the acting troupe of Montdory. Mélite represents Corneille’s creation of a new genre, the comedy of manners, which was a departure from the coarse or buffoonish farce in vogue at the time.

Ahmad Nami (1873 – 1962) was the 5th Prime Minister and 2nd President of Syria from 1926 to 1928. Born in Beirut to an affluent family with ties to the Ottoman dynasty, he was of Turkish and Circassian origin.

The National Audit Office of Tanzania (NAOT) is an independent parliamentary body responsible for auditing the Government of Tanzania. It is headquartered in Dodoma and was formed in 2001, succeeding the Exchequer and Audit Department. The NAOT's mandate is derived from the Public Finance Act of 2001, revised in 2008. The current Auditor General of Tanzania is Charles Kichere.

*ER* featured a large ensemble cast that changed throughout its 15-season run. The original starring cast included Anthony Edwards, George Clooney, Sherry Stringfield, Noah Wyle, and Eriq La Salle. Julianna Margulies joined the main cast after guest-starring in the pilot.
Marie Fillunger (27 January 1850 – 23 December 1930) was an Austrian singer, and the longtime partner of Eugenie Schumann, who was a daughter of Robert and Clara Schumann.

The Slinkard Fire was a wildfire that started on August 29, 2017, west of Topaz, California, due to lightning. It burned 8,925 acres (36 km2) in Mono, Alpine, and Douglas counties, costing $1.9 million in damages. It was fully contained on September 12, 2017.

*Riffs* is a live album by alto saxophonist Jimmy Lyons, released in 1982. It was recorded at Le Dreher club in Paris on September 13–14, 1980, and features Lyons with bassoonist Karen Borca, bassist Jay Oliver, and drummer Paul Murphy. The album contains lengthy versions of "Theme" and "Riffs #1/II".

The Fine Arts Department (Thai: กรมศิลปากร) is a government agency of Thailand under the Ministry of Culture. It was formed in 1912 and is responsible for the study and management of cultural heritage.

Jean-Philippe Mateta (born 28 June 1997) is a French professional footballer who plays as a striker for Premier League club Crystal Palace. He has represented France internationally at U19 and U21 youth levels.

Omsk hemorrhagic fever (OHF) is a viral infectious disease caused by the Omsk hemorrhagic fever virus (OHFV). The virus is transmitted to humans via infected ticks or contact with infected animals, and it is found in Western Siberia.

In mathematics, Betti numbers are topological invariants that describe the number of holes of each dimension in a topological space. For a non-negative integer *k*, the *k*th Betti number *b<sub>k</sub>*(X) of a space X is defined as the rank of the *k*th homology group of X. In simpler terms, *b<sub>0</sub>* counts the number of connected components, *b<sub>1</sub>* counts the number of one-dimensional or "circular" holes, *b<sub>2</sub>* counts the number of two-dimensional "voids" or "cavities", and so on.

Betti numbers can be formally defined using homology groups, which are algebraic objects that capture the connectivity of a topological space. The *k*th Betti number is the rank of the *k*th homology group, which represents the number of *k*-dimensional cycles that are not boundaries of (*k*+1)-dimensional objects.

The Poincaré polynomial is a generating function that encodes the Betti numbers of a topological space. For example, the Poincaré polynomial of the torus is 1 + 2*t* + *t*<sup>2</sup>, reflecting its Betti numbers *b<sub>0</sub>*=1, *b<sub>1</sub>*=2, and *b<sub>2</sub>*=1. Betti numbers have applications in various fields, including graph theory, simplicial complexes, and the study of manifolds. They are also related to the Euler characteristic and the dimensions of spaces of differential forms.

Associated Equipment Company (AEC) was a British vehicle manufacturer that built buses, motorcoaches and trucks from 1912 until 1979. The name Associated Equipment Company was hardly ever used; instead it traded under the AEC and ACLO brands.

During World War One, AEC was the most prolific British lorry manufacturer; after building London's buses before the great war.

Aloysius "Al" Piechota (January 19, 1914 – June 13, 1996) was an American professional baseball pitcher. He played in Major League Baseball (MLB) for the Boston Bees/Braves from 1940 to 1941.

Piechota began his professional career in 1933, leading the Mississippi Valley League in wins and winning a league championship. He later played for the Newark Bears and Kansas City Blues, winning another pennant with the latter. After his MLB career, Piechota served in the United States military during World War II, even pitching against the Chicago Cubs while stationed in Indiana. Following his discharge, he returned to the minor leagues until 1951. After baseball, Piechota was a police officer for the Chicago Police Department for 25 years.

The Aleutian tern (Onychoprion aleuticus) is a seabird in the family Laridae. It is a medium-sized tern, measuring 32–39 cm (13–15 in) long, with a 75–80 cm (30–31 in) wingspan. The breeding adult has a white forehead, black cap, mid-grey mantle, and white rump and tail.

The Aleutian tern breeds along the Pacific coastlines of Alaska and Russia. It is strongly migratory, with its wintering range believed to be off Indonesia and Malaysia. The species is listed as "Vulnerable" due to rapid population declines. Threats include habitat modification, predation, and human disturbance. Conservation efforts include monitoring populations and identifying migration pathways. Aleutian terns breed in colonies and are site-faithful. They primarily feed on small fish, crustaceans, and insects, foraging by swooping down to take food from the surface of the water. They are highly social birds, often nesting in mixed-species colonies.
*Sussex Archaeological Collections* is an annual journal relating to the archaeology and history of Sussex. It has been published by the Sussex Archaeological Society since 1848.

Lala Hsu (traditional Chinese: 徐佳瑩; simplified Chinese: 徐佳莹; pinyin: Xú Jiāyíng; Pe̍h-ōe-jī: Chhî Ka-êng; born 20 December 1984) is a Taiwanese singer-songwriter. Hsu was born in Hualien County, Taiwan, and grew up in Taichung.

Hsu was a contestant in the third season of CTV's One Million Star in 2008. She won the contest with a self-composed and written song on 15 August of that year. She released her self-titled debut album on 29 May 2009. She has since published five albums to date; Lala Hsu in 2009, Limits in 2010, Ideal Life in 2012, Missing in 2014 and The Inner Me in 2017.

In 2010, Hsu won the Best New Artist award at the 21st Golden Melody Awards for her eponymously named album, Lala Hsu, becoming the first One Million Star alumni to win a Golden Melody Award. In 2015, she was nominated in six categories at the 26th Golden Melody Awards for the album Missing.

In 2016, Hsu was a participant of the fourth season of I Am a Singer which gained her wider attention in mainland China. A significant rise in popularity lead to numerous invitations to perform the theme songs of film and television works.

In 2018, Hsu was named Best Mandarin Female Singer at the 29th Golden Melody Awards.

Hawthorn railway station is located in Hawthorn, Victoria, Australia, 6.89 km from Southern Cross station. It is serviced by Lilydale, Belgrave, and Alamein line services. It opened on April 13, 1861.

Grange Barn is a historic timber-framed building in Coggeshall, Essex, England. Grange Barn was built by the Cistercians in the 13th century to serve Coggeshall Abbey. It underwent significant structural alteration in the 14th century. It is Grade I listed.

The barn is 36.57 metres long, 13.71 metres wide and 10.67 metres high. For comparison, England's largest medieval barn, Harmondsworth Great Barn, is 58.55 metres long, 11.3 metres wide, and 11.9 metres high. The barn has a nave and two aisles. There are two midstreys (gabled porches) which would have provided access for wagons: these are 18th century.

The Yale Glee Club is a mixed chorus of men and women, consisting of students of Yale University in New Haven, Connecticut. Founded in 1861, it is the third oldest collegiate chorus in the United States after the Harvard Glee Club, founded in 1858, and the University of Michigan Men's Glee Club, founded in 1859. The Glee Club performs several concerts each year in New Haven and goes on tour each January. According to music critic Zachary Woolfe of the New York Times, it is "one of the best collegiate singing ensembles, and one of the most adventurous." Its members are "world famous for their harmonic precision" per New York Times music critic Robert Sherman.

John James Hughes (1814 – 1889) was a Welsh engineer and businessman. He founded the city of Donetsk, Ukraine, formerly Hughesovka, in 1869 after accepting a concession from the Imperial Russian Government to develop metal works in the region. He is buried in West Norwood Cemetery in London.

Matching logic is a formal system that operates on patterns, where statements evaluate to the set of values that "match" them. It is used with reachability logic by the K Framework to specify operational semantics and create Hoare logic. Matching logic can be converted to first-order logic.

Michael Rowan "Mick" O'Driscoll (born 8 October 1978) is an Irish former rugby union player. A lock, he played for Munster and USA Perpignan, winning 23 caps for Ireland. He was a member of Ireland's 2009 Grand Slam winning team.

True Liberty is an American Christian hardcore band, and they primarily play hardcore punk and punk rock. They come from Richmond, Virginia. The band started making music in 2005, and their members are lead vocalist and lead guitarist, Mike Liberty, bassist, Tom Liberty, and drummer and background vocalist, Aaron Liberty. Their first studio album, This Is War, was released in 2007 by Raging Storm Records. They independently released, Brotherhood, that came out in 2008. The studio album, City of the One, was released by Wounded Records, in 2010. Their subsequent studio album, Marked for Life, was released by Thumper Punk Records, in 2012. They released a live album, Give Me True Liberty or Give Me Death, with Thumper Punk Records, in 2014.

The C. H. Moore House, also known as the C. H. Moore Homestead DeWitt County Museum, is a historic house museum in Clinton, Illinois. Built in 1865 in the Second Empire and Italianate styles, it was added to the National Register of Historic Places in 1979.
Bing Yeh (Chinese: 葉炳輝; pinyin: Yè Bǐnghuī; Wade–Giles: Yeh Ping-hui; born c. 1950) is a Taiwanese-American electrical engineer and business executive.

The Aguapeí State Park (Portuguese: Parque Estadual do Aguapeí) is a state park in the state of São Paulo, Brazil. Created in 1998, it protects 9,044 hectares (22,350 acres) of the Atlantic Forest biome.

ER is an American drama series that aired on NBC from September 19, 1994, until April 2, 2009. The series featured a large ensemble cast that changed dramatically over its long run. The main cast was augmented by a wealth of recurring characters and award-winning guest stars.

Maya Aleksandrovna Ulanovskaya (also known as Maiia Ulanovskie and Maria Ulanovsky) (Russian: Майя Александровна Улановская) (Hebrew: מאיה אולאנובסקאיה) (October 20, 1932 – June 25, 2020), was an American-born Russian-Israeli who, with spouse Anatoly Yakobson, participated in the dissident movement in the USSR and became a professor, writer, and translator in Israel.

Wilson's snipe (Gallinago delicata) is a small, stocky shorebird. The genus name gallinago is New Latin for a woodcock or snipe from Latin gallina, "hen" and the suffix -ago, "resembling". The specific delicata is Latin for "dainty".

This species was considered to be a subspecies of the common snipe (G. gallinago) until 2003 when it was given its own species status, though not all authorities recognized this immediately. Wilson's snipe differs from the latter species in having a narrower white trailing edge to the wings, and eight pairs of tail feathers instead of the typical seven of the common snipe. Its common name commemorates the American ornithologist Alexander Wilson.

Valmiera (pronunciation; Lang-de; Lang-pl see other names) is the largest city of the historical Vidzeme region, Latvia, with a total area of 19.35 square kilometres (7 square miles). As of 2002, Valmiera had a population of 27,323, and in 2020 – 24 879. It is a state city the seat of Valmiera Municipality.

Valmiera lies at the crossroads of several important roads, 107 kilometres (66 miles) to the north-east from Riga, the capital of Latvia, and 50 km (31 mi) south of the border with Estonia. Valmiera lies on both banks of the Gauja River.

Winter Haven Hospital is a hospital and health service system based in Winter Haven, Florida. The hospital is managed and supported by the Winter Haven Hospital Foundation—a 501(c)3 non-profit organization—and is owned by BayCare Health System, a group of 15 not-for-profit hospitals in the Tampa Bay Area. The Winter Haven Hospital system includes the hospital proper, the Community Blood Center, the Regency Medical Center for Women and Infants, the Winter Haven Ambulatory Surgery and Diagnostic Center, the Center for Behavioral Health, and seven Family Health Centers in Winter Haven and surrounding communities.

Frederick Rodney Holt (born 1934) is an American computer engineer and political activist. He is Apple employee #5, and developed the unique power supply for the 1977 Apple II. Actor Ron Eldard portrayed him in the 2013 film, Jobs.

Nathan Sawaya (born July 10, 1973) is an American artist who builds custom three-dimensional sculptures and large-scale mosaics from popular everyday items and is best known for his work with standard LEGO building bricks.

Al-Azhar Park is a public park located in Cairo, Egypt. Situated in Medieval Cairo, the 30-hectare park was created in May 2005 by the Aga Khan Trust for Culture's Historic Cities Support Programme, at a cost of $30 million. During construction, the Ayyubid wall was discovered and restored, along with the revitalization of the adjacent Darb al-Ahmar neighborhood. An Urban Plaza is under development at the park's northern end, which will house the Museum of Historic Cairo.

Mild androgen insensitivity syndrome (MAIS) is a condition that results in a mild impairment of the cell's ability to respond to androgens. The degree of impairment is sufficient to impair spermatogenesis and / or the development of secondary sexual characteristics at puberty in males, but does not affect genital differentiation or development. Female genital and sexual development is not significantly affected by the insensitivity to androgens; as such, MAIS is only diagnosed in males. The clinical phenotype associated with MAIS is a normal male habitus with mild spermatogenic defect and / or reduced secondary terminal hair.

MAIS is one of three types of androgen insensitivity syndrome, which is divided into three categories that are differentiated by the degree of genital masculinization: complete androgen insensitivity syndrome (CAIS) is indicated when the external genitalia is phenotypically female, mild androgen insensitivity syndrome (MAIS) is indicated when the external genitalia is phenotypically male, and partial androgen insensitivity syndrome (PAIS) is indicated when the external genitalia is partially, but not fully masculinized.

Androgen insensitivity syndrome is the largest single entity that leads to 46,XY undermasculinization.

Alan Arnett McLeod, VC (20 April 1899 – 6 November 1918) was a Canadian soldier, aviator, and a recipient of the Victoria Cross, the highest award for gallantry in the face of the enemy that can be awarded to British and Commonwealth forces. McLeod served as a pilot in the Royal Flying Corps and later the Royal Air Force during the First World War.

Ronaldo Soares Giovanelli (born 1967) is a Brazilian former goalkeeper. He played for Corinthians from 1986 to 1998, winning the Campeonato Brasileiro in 1990. He was also the lead singer of the band Ronaldo e os Impedidos.

XBase++ is a Clipper-syntax compiler created after Computer Associates abandoned Clipper. It supports the Replaceable Database Drivers (RDD) of Clipper through DatabaseEngines (DBEs), including DBF, FOX, NTX, CDX, SDF, and DEL. XBase++ also supports the @SAY/GET commands, a graphic editor, a visual development environment, an integrated debugger, and a resource compiler. It can generate EXE or DLL files.
Ronaldo Soares Giovanelli (born 20 November 1967) is a Brazilian football pundit and retired footballer. Although as a child he was a forward, he spent his career as a goalkeeper.

The Great Sandy Desert is a desert in Western Australia and the Northern Territory. It spans 284,993 square kilometers (110,036 sq mi) and contains large ergs of longitudinal dunes. The region is sparsely populated, consisting of Indigenous Australian communities and mining centers. Rainfall is low but high for a desert, with high temperatures in the summer and warm temperatures in the winter. The vegetation is dominated by spinifex and populated by a variety of animals.

The National Hockey Stadium was a sports stadium in Milton Keynes with a nominal capacity of around 4,000 seats: this was temporarily increased to 9,000 between 2003 and 2007. It was used by England Hockey as their national stadium from 1995 to 2003 and then as a professional football stadium by Wimbledon FC, rebranded as Milton Keynes Dons in 2004.

In summer 2007, the Dons relocated to the new Stadium MK, near Bletchley, leaving the hockey stadium without a tenant. It was unused for two years and, in late 2009, demolition began in preparation for the redevelopment of the site into the new Network Rail headquarters which became operational in July 2012.

The 2014 FedEx Cup Playoffs was the series of four golf tournaments that determined the season champion on the U.S.-based PGA Tour. It was played from August 21 to September 14 and comprised the following four events:

These were the eighth FedEx Cup playoffs since their inception in 2007.

The point distributions can be seen here.
Boulos Fahmy Eskandar (b. 1 January 1957) is a Egyptian lawyer and the current President of the Supreme Constitutional Court of Egypt.

An Ziwen (Chinese: 安子文; pinyin: Ān Zǐwén; 25 September 1909 – 25 June 1980), born as An Zhihan (安之瀚), was a Chinese politician and member of the Central Committee of the Chinese Communist Party. He served as minister of the CCP Central Committee Organization Department, the Central People's Government Minister of Personnel, deputy secretary of the Central Discipline Inspection Commission, the Standing Committee of the CPPCC National Committee, among other roles. He authored the book "Revolutionary successors training is a strategic task of the party".

Helene Hewitt is a British climate modeler at the Met Office. She studied mathematics at the University of Cambridge and earned a doctorate from the University of Southampton. Hewitt is Head of Ocean and Sea Ice model development at the Met Office and coordinated the IPCC Sixth Assessment Report. She was appointed an Order of the British Empire in 2022.

The New Zealand Superstock Championship is an annually contested national championship for superstocks on a rotating track schedule. The current champion is Asher Rees, registered to Kihikihi Speedway, who won his first title at Paradise Valley Raceway, near Rotorua, in January 2021.

Graham Stretch was the inaugural champion, winning the title at Palmerston North International Speedway(Central Energy Trust Arena) in the 1965/66 season. Craig Boote and Kevin Free have been the most successful winners, each winning 3 times.

Maya Aleksandrovna Ulanovskaya (1932–2020) was a Soviet and Israeli literary critic, translator, and teacher. Born in New York City to Soviet spies, she was later arrested in the USSR for anti-Stalinist activities and sentenced to the Gulag. After release and emigration to Israel, she translated works from English, Hebrew, and Yiddish into Russian.

The Associated Equipment Company (AEC) was a British vehicle manufacturer founded in 1912 as a subsidiary of the London General Omnibus Company (LGOC). AEC produced buses, lorries, and motorcoaches at its Southall, England headquarters until 1979, when it was closed down by its parent company, British Leyland.

Jeff Danielson (born August 6, 1970) is an American politician and firefighter from Waterloo, Iowa. A member of the Democratic Party, he served in the Iowa Senate from 2005 to 2019, representing the 10th district until redistricting in 2013, after which he represented the 30th district. Danielson was first elected in 2004 and re-elected in 2008 and 2012. He also served as President Pro Tempore of the Iowa Senate from 2006 to 2012.

Danielson is a veteran of the United States Navy and served as Vice-Chair of the Veterans Affairs committee. He resigned from the Senate in early 2019 and became a lobbyist for the American Wind Energy Association, which led to a special election.

Petro Pysarchuk (born June 6, 1955) is a Ukrainian politician and entrepreneur. He was a member of the Verhovna Rada (Ukrainian Parliament) from 2002 to 2005, from 2006 to 2007, and from 2007 to 2012.
London Bourne (1793–1869) was a former Barbadian slave who became a wealthy merchant and abolitionist.
David William Heneker (31 March 1906 – 30 January 2001) was a writer and composer of British popular music and musicals, best known for creating the music and lyrics for Half a Sixpence.

World Wide Adventures was a series of live-action short films distributed by a major animation studio after it curtailed its own live-action production in 1957. While most were independent travelogues, a few were supervised by in-house producers. Largely forgotten for decades, some have been revived as "extras" on Turner Classic Movies. Information on the series is incomplete due to a lack of coverage at the time of release.

The Rekolokomotives of DR Class 52.80 first appeared in 1960 in service with the Deutsche Reichsbahn in East Germany as extensive rebuilds of the wartime locomotives or Kriegslokomotiven of the DRB Class 52 built by Nazi Germany. This modernisation, described as 'reconstruction' (Rekonstruktion, hence Rekolokomotive), extended to almost all of the components and systems on the engine.

"Let It Go" is a 2010 jazz/canto-jazz single by Jacky Cheung, from the album *Private Corner*. Written by Roxanne Seeman, Daniel Lindstrom, and Daniele Musto, with Cantonese lyrics by Kenny So, it was produced by Andrew Tuason. The song reached number 16 on the HMVHK sales chart. Cheung performed "Let It Go" on his "Private Corner" Mini-Concert and his "1/2 Century Tour".

ESET NOD32 Antivirus is an antivirus software suite created by the Slovak company ESET. First released in 1987, NOD32 was originally named NOD-ICE and was DOS-based. ESET also offers ESET Smart Security, ESET Mobile Security, ESET Remote Administrator and ESET SysInspector, among other programs.

Lieutenant General Sir Daril Watson, GCB, CBE, MC (1888–1967) was a British Army officer who served in both World War I and World War II. He later became Quartermaster-General to the Forces before retiring in 1947.

Monarchs, Rulers, Dynasties and Kingdoms of the World: An Encyclopaedic Guide to More Than 13,000 Rulers and 1,000 Dynasties from 3000 BC to the 20th Century is a non-fiction work by R.F. Tapsell, published in 1983. It is a comprehensive record of kings, queens, sultans, and emperors all in a single volume. It includes many dynasties that are rarely described except in advanced studies of individual countries or regions. The relationship of each monarch to his or her predecessor is provided which enables the reader to construct a rudimentary family tree.

Baaji (‹See RfD› transl. elder sister) is a 2019 Pakistani romantic drama film, directed by Saqib Malik and produced by his Production company Page 33 Films. The film features Meera as Shameera Osman Khalid Butt as Rohail Khan/ Khalid Osman, Amna Ilyas as Neha, Mohsin Abbas Haider as Ajji, Ali Kazmi as Rammy, Nayyar Ejaz, and Nisho in pivotal roles. It is a dramatic tale of a fading Lollywood actress Shameera (Meera), who is not accepting that now her position in the industry is not much valuable. The story revolves around the relationship of Shameera with a Salon girl Neha (Amna Ilyas) who later becomes an assistant of Shameera and make this opportunity happens of working with an American Based Pakistani Director Rohail Khan (Osman Khalid Butt). Neha who belongs to a conservative lower middle class family then also make herself involved in Shameera's comeback movie titled Waapsi by helping them in rehearsals and script making so far she get a chance to take a supporting role in the movie. The film includes Ali Kazmi as Raamy (Shameera's Boyfriend) who is highly aggressive about Shameera. Mohsin Abbas Haider plays the love interest of neha's character, while the Legendary actor Nayyer Ejaz supports the film by his great comics and unforgettable role as Chand Kamal, film also includes numerous well-known faces from the industry as in cameo appearances.
The Weatherly 201 is an agricultural aircraft designed and manufactured by the Weatherly Aviation Company. It is a low-wing monoplane certified in 1967.

Alexander Ignatyevich Tarasov-Rodionov (Lang-ru; October 7, 1885 – September 3, 1938) was a Russian/Soviet writer and revolutionary, best known for his novel "Chocolate" which at the time of publication was acclaimed as a tale of heroic self-sacrifice but has since been criticized as a justification for the Red Terror.
