A zograscope is an optical device for magnifying flat pictures that also has the property of enhancing the sense of the depth shown in the picture. It consists of a large magnifying lens through which the picture is viewed. Devices containing only the lens are sometimes referred to as graphoscopes. Other models have the lens mounted on a stand in front of an angled mirror. This allows someone to sit at a table and to look through the lens at the picture flat on the table. Pictures viewed in this way need to be left-right reversed; this is obvious in the case of writing. A print made for this purpose, typically with extensive graphical projection perspective, is called a vue d'optique or "perspective view".

Zograscopes were popular during the later half of the 18th century as parlour entertainments. Most existing ones from that time are fine furniture, with turned stands, mouldings, brass fittings, and fine finishes.

According to Michael Quinion, the origin of the term is lost, but it is also known as a diagonal mirror, as an optical pillar machine, or as an optical diagonal machine.

In Japan, the zograscope became known as 和蘭眼鏡 (Oranda megane, 'Dutch glasses') or 覗き眼鏡 (nozoki megane 'peeping glasses'), and the pictures were known as 眼鏡絵 (megane-e, 'optique picture') 繰絵 (karakuri-e 'tricky picture').

Barysaw is a city in Belarus, located in the Minsk Voblast. Founded in 1102, it was part of the Grand Duchy of Lithuania from the late 13th century until 1793, when it became part of the Russian Empire.

Formula One 99 is a racing video game developed by Studio 33 and published by Psygnosis for PlayStation and Microsoft Windows. It is the sequel to the 1998 video game Formula 1 98 and was based on the 1999 Formula One World Championship.

Following the disappointment of Formula 1 98, and the subsequent split with Visual Science, Psygnosis hired Studio 33 to develop Formula One 99, after their successful development of Newman/Haas Racing the previous year. During production of the game, Sony completed a full acquisition of Psygnosis, making this the final Formula One game to be published under the Psygnosis name.

The game is unique in having substitute drivers appear in the game as they did in the real 1999 season (such as Mika Salo replacing Michael Schumacher for the races between Austria and Europe). A new grid editor tool was also introduced, allowing players to customise the starting grid to their own liking before a race. Despite the lack of an arcade mode that had featured in previous titles, the game was widely praised as an overwhelming improvement in comparison to the 1998 game.

Edward A. Keenan (October 15, 1894 – April 12, 1970) was an American politician who served as the 34th mayor of Burlington, Vermont from 1963 to 1965. Prior to being mayor he served on the Burlington Board of Aldermen from 1959 to 1963.

The Liberators' civil war (43–42 BC) was a series of battles fought between the Second Triumvirate and the Liberatores, following Julius Caesar's assassination. The Liberatores, led by Brutus and Cassius, controlled the Eastern Roman provinces and allied kingdoms. The Triumvirate, led by Mark Antony and Octavian, sought to avenge Caesar's death and gain control. The conflict culminated in the Battle of Philippi, resulting in the defeat of the Liberatores and the Triumvirate's control over the Roman Republic.

Laura Natalia Esquivel (born May 18, 1994, in Buenos Aires, Argentina), known professionally as Laura Esquivel, is an Argentine and Italian actress and singer, who gained international popularity for her debut acting role as Patricia "Patito" Castro in the popular Argentine children's telenovela, Patito Feo.

The Hiller YH-32 Hornet (company designation HJ-1) was an American ultralight helicopter built by Hiller Aircraft in the early 1950s. It was a small and unique design because it was powered by two Hiller 8RJ2B ramjet engines mounted on the rotor blade tips which weigh 13 lb (5.9 kg) each and deliver an equivalent of 45 hp (34 kW) for a total of 90 hp (67 kW). Versions of the HJ-1 Hornet were built for the United States Army and the United States Navy in the early 1950s.

The Hiller Museum identifies the YH-32A, named the Sally Rand, as the first helicopter gunship.

Nathan's Famous, Inc. is an American company that operates a chain of fast food restaurants specializing in hot dogs. The original Nathan's restaurant stands at the corner of Surf and Stillwell Avenues in the Coney Island neighborhood of the Brooklyn borough of New York City, New York. The company's headquarters are at One Jericho Plaza in Jericho, New York, part of Oyster Bay, New York.

Miniclip SA is a Swiss online game website and subsidiary launched in 2001. It is known for hosting a large collection of browser games, particularly Flash games. Miniclip has also developed and published mobile games for various platforms, including iOS, Android, and Windows Phone. Some of their popular mobile titles include 8 Ball Pool, Golf Battle, Gravity Guy, and Agar.io.

In 2012, Miniclip began distributing games on the Xbox division of Windows 8, with titles like Gravity Guy, iStunt 2, and Monster Island. In 2017, they released their first game compatible with Xbox One, PC, and PlayStation 4, titled MX Nitro. Miniclip was acquired by Tencent in 2015. The company has faced some security concerns, with reports of malicious software being distributed through downloads associated with the site in the mid-2000s.
Ya'akov Ekhoiz (born 1957) is an Israeli former football defender and coach.

*When Hope Calls* is an American family drama Western series created by Alfonso H. Moreno, inspired by *When Calls the Heart*. The series follows orphaned sisters Lillian (Morgan Kohan) and Grace (Jocelyn Hudon) in 1916 as they reunite and open an orphanage in the Canadian town of Brookfield.

The series, a spin-off of *When Calls the Heart*, premiered on Hallmark Movies Now in August 2019. In 2021, it was renewed for a second season and moved to GAC Family. Lori Loughlin reprised her role from *When Calls the Heart* in the season premiere.

Daniel J. Berthiaume (born January 26, 1966) is a Canadian former professional ice hockey goaltender who played six seasons in the National Hockey League (NHL) with the Winnipeg Jets, Minnesota North Stars, Los Angeles Kings, Boston Bruins, and Ottawa Senators.
*Nina* is a French television series about a nurse working in a hospital.

*Spindizzy* is an action and puzzle game developed by Paul Shirley of Electric Dreams Software and released in 1986. Played from an isometric perspective, the player controls a probe called GERALD to explore interconnected stages within a time limit.

Inspired by Ultimate Play the Game titles and *Marble Madness*, Shirley used a script to generate numerous levels within a small storage space. Originally released for Commodore 64 and Amstrad CPC, it was later ported to other platforms. Activision published the game in the United States.

*Spindizzy* was well-received for its visuals and design, with some criticism aimed at its audio. A sequel, *Spindizzy Worlds*, was released in 1990.

*The Ladies of the House* is a 2014 American horror film directed by John Stuart Wildman. It follows three men who are invited into the home of a stripper and her roommates, only to discover that the women are cannibalistic and intend to kill them. The film premiered at the Dallas International Film Festival on April 4, 2014, and received generally positive reviews from horror-focused websites.
General Sir Daril Gerard Watson GCB CBE MC (17 October 1888 − 1 July 1967) was a senior British Army officer who saw service during both World War I and World War II.
Kylie Nicole Sicangco Padilla (born January 25, 1993) is a Filipina actress, singer, and model. She is best known for her role as Amihan in *Encantadia* (2016).

The 2020–21 UEFA Women's Champions League knockout phase was played from 9 December 2020 to 16 May 2021. Thirty-two teams from twenty-one associations competed in the knockout phase. The final was held at Gamla Ullevi in Gothenburg. The top scorers were Jennifer Hermoso and Fran Kirby, with six goals each.

The National Audit Office (NAO) of Tanzania is an independent Parliamentary body in Tanzania which is responsible for auditing central government departments, government agencies and non-departmental public bodies. The NAO also carries out value for money (VFM) audits into the administration of public policy. The office is headed by the Chief Auditor General appointed by the President of Tanzania.

In algebraic topology, the Betti numbers are used to distinguish topological spaces based on the connectivity of n-dimensional simplicial complexes. For the most reasonable finite-dimensional spaces (such as compact manifolds, finite simplicial complexes or CW complexes), the sequence of Betti numbers is 0 from some point onward (Betti numbers vanish above the dimension of a space), and they are all finite.

The nth Betti number represents the rank of the nth homology group, denoted Hn, which tells us the maximum number of cuts that can be made before separating a surface into two pieces or 0-cycles, 1-cycles, etc. For example, if then, if then, if then, if then, etc. Note that only the ranks of infinite groups are considered, so for example if, where is the finite cyclic group of order 2, then. These finite components of the homology groups are their torsion subgroups, and they are denoted by torsion coefficients.

The term "Betti numbers" was coined by Henri Poincaré after Enrico Betti. The modern formulation is due to Emmy Noether. Betti numbers are used today in fields such as simplicial homology, computer science, digital images, etc.

The American Federation of Aviculture (AFA) is an educational organization founded in 1974 to represent bird clubs and protect aviculture. Based in Austin, Texas, the AFA's mission is to promote aviculture through education, improved breeding and husbandry practices, conservation, research, and legislative awareness.

The AFA hosts an annual convention featuring speakers on aviculture topics. It publishes the AFA Watchbird magazine and other publications, and offers a two-level certification program called Fundamentals of Aviculture. The AFA also distributes grants for research and wild parrot conservation projects. Its legislative committee monitors legislation that may impact the keeping and breeding of companion parrots. The organization has eleven geographically segmented regions of members.

Dau al set was a Catalan avant-garde magazine founded in September 1948 by Joan-Josep Tharrats and directed by Joan Ponç. The magazine was produced by the artistic group Dau al Set. It was the continuation of another magazine called Algol (magazine) published by the same group. The magazine had eight pages measuring 250 mm × 175 mm (9.8 in × 6.9 in). The texts were written in Catalan and Spanish. The magazine continued publishing numbers until December 1951.

Martyrdom in Judaism refers to the concept of Jews sacrificing their lives for their faith, a notion rooted in the Hebrew Bible and central to Jewish religious thought. The Binding of Isaac is considered an archetypal example, where Isaac's willingness to be sacrificed is seen as a model for future generations.

Throughout Jewish history, individuals who died while defending their faith or sanctifying God's name (Kiddush Hashem) have been regarded as martyrs. This includes soldiers killed in a Milkhemet Mitzvah ("war by commandment") and those who chose death rather than betray their beliefs.

Biblical figures such as Samson, King Saul, and Zechariah ben Jehoiada are considered martyrs. The destruction of the First and Second Temples, along with the subsequent massacres and exiles, resulted in countless Jewish deaths, commemorated through fast days and mourning periods.

The Maccabean Revolt saw numerous Jews martyred for resisting Hellenization. During the Jewish-Roman Wars, over a million Jews perished, and the killing of the Ten Martyrs is a solemn part of Yom Kippur liturgy.

Later periods, including the Crusades, the Spanish Inquisition, the Khmelnytsky Uprising, and the Black Death, witnessed widespread anti-Jewish violence and martyrdom. The six million Jews murdered in the Holocaust are considered martyrs, and memorialized on Yom HaShoah. Even today, victims of antisemitic attacks are regarded as martyrs who died al Kiddush Hashem.

Frederick Rodney "Rod" Holt (born 1934) is an American engineer. He is best known for developing the power supply for the Apple II computer. Holt joined Apple Computer as employee #5 and later became Vice President of Engineering.

"Flash" (stylized as "FLASH") is a single by Perfume, released on March 16, 2016. It is the fifth single from their fifth studio album Cosmic Explorer. The single reached first place in the real-time ranking of iTunes with 200,000+ downloads. It is also the band's highest selling single since "Love the World".

The single was used as the theme song for the live action adapted film Chihayafuru.

Seanad Éireann sidebar This is a list of the members of the 22nd Seanad Éireann, the upper house of the Oireachtas (legislature) of Ireland. These Senators were elected or appointed in 2002, after the 2002 general election and served until the close of poll for the 23rd Seanad at the end of July 2007.

Password synchronization is a process that allows users to use the same password across multiple systems, such as on an enterprise network. This simplifies password management for users, reducing the need to remember numerous passwords, which can lead to stronger password choices and fewer help desk calls.

However, password synchronization introduces security risks. If the single, synchronized password is compromised, all systems sharing that password become vulnerable. This is similar to the risk in single sign-on and password vault solutions, where compromise of the master password grants access to all associated systems.

Password synchronization can be triggered by a password change on any of the synchronized systems or centrally through the synchronization software. Some systems may copy password hashes if the hashing algorithm is the same across systems, but often access to the plaintext password is required.

Rami Mehmed Pasha (1645–1706) was an Ottoman statesman and poet who served as Grand Vizier (1703) and governor of Cyprus and of Egypt (1704–06). He was known as a poet of divan literature (the epithet Rami, meaning "Obedient", is his pen name in his poems).

Rosa Amelia Guzmán de Araujo was a Salvadoran feminist and activist. She co-founded the journal *Tribuna Femenina* in 1945 and was a representative of the Liga Femenina Salvadoreña (LFS). Guzmán successfully advocated for women's suffrage in El Salvador in 1950. In 1956, she became one of the first women elected to the Legislative Assembly of El Salvador.

The 2021 Little League World Series was held from August 19 to August 29 at the Little League headquarters complex in South Williamsport, Pennsylvania. Due to the COVID-19 pandemic, the event was limited to US-based teams only. The most recent prior edition of the tournament contested without international teams had been 1975. Taylor North Little League of Taylor, Michigan, defeated West Side Little League of Hamilton, Ohio, in the championship by a 5–2 score. It was the first championship for a team from Michigan since 1959.

On August 13, it was announced that tickets would not be distributed to the public due to concerns over the Delta variant, with spectators limited to invited friends and family of teams, and other high-level supporters.

Coventry RLFC was a rugby league club formed in 1910. They played for three seasons before folding due to financial difficulties. Rugby league returned to Coventry in the 21st century with the Coventry Bears.

A composite film is a feature film whose screenplay is composed of two or more distinct stories. More generally, composite structure refers to an aesthetic principle in which the narrative structure relies on contiguity and linking rather than linearity. In a composite text or film, individual pieces are complete within themselves, yet they form a whole work that is greater than the sum of its individual parts.

Ja Lama (1862 – 1922) was a guerrilla leader of Kalmyk origin who fought for Mongolian independence in the early 20th century. Born Dambiijantsan in the Astrakhan region of Russia, he claimed Russian citizenship while campaigning against Qing rule in Mongolia. He helped defeat the Manchus at Khovd in 1912 and was granted noble titles. However, his autocratic rule led to his arrest in 1914. After returning to Mongolia, he operated independently, defying both the Bogd Khan and later the communist government, until his assassination in 1922.

The Parapetí River is a 500 km (310 mi) river in Bolivia. It arises in the Cordillera Central and flows into the Bañados de Izozog wetlands. The river and wetlands are a Ramsar Convention site, designated in 2001. Part of the wetlands are within the Kaa-Iya del Gran Chaco National Park.

Robert Neelly Bellah (1927–2013) was an American sociologist and academic. He taught at Harvard and the University of California, Berkeley. Bellah is known for his work on American civil religion and his book, *Habits of the Heart*.

Woo In-hee (Korean: 우인희, died 1981) was a North Korean actress and a mistress of Kim Jong-il.

Having reached stardom in the 1960s and 1970, Woo, renowned for her beauty, acted in dozens of films. She married Yoo Hosun, a famed film director, but she was involved in affairs outside of her marriage. In the late 1970s, she became a secret mistress of Kim Jong-il. After Woo began an affair with another man, Kim had her publicly executed in front of 6,000 people.
Ukhra Assembly constituency was an assembly constituency in what is now Paschim Bardhaman district in the Indian state of West Bengal. It covered the Ukhra area.

"Al-Damad" Ahmad Nami or "Damat" Ahmet Nami (Lang-ar;‎ 1873 – 13 December 1962) was an Ottoman prince (damat), the fifth prime minister of Syria and second president of Syria (1926–28), and a lecturer of history and politics.

*Dulce Enemiga* is a Venezuelan telenovela that aired on Venevisión between 1995 and 1996, starring Gigi Zanchetta, Guillermo Dávila, and Natalia Streignard. The series follows Victoria, who seeks revenge on Julio Cesar for his role in her father's death, but ends up falling in love with him.
The Aguapeí State Park (Lang-pt) is a state park in the state of São Paulo, Brazil. It protects an area of floodplain in the Atlantic Forest biome, home to many water birds.

Nick Rotteveel (born January 6, 1989), most famously known as Nicky Romero, is a Dutch DJ, record producer, and remixer. He has released tracks on labels such as Spinnin' Records and Revealed Recordings, and founded his own label, Protocol Recordings, in 2012. Romero was ranked number 7 on *DJ Mag's* Top 100 DJs in 2013.

The handscroll is a long, narrow, horizontal scroll format in East Asia used for calligraphy or paintings. A handscroll usually measures up to several meters in length and around 25–40 cm in height. Handscrolls are generally viewed starting from the right end. This kind of scroll is intended to be read or viewed flat on a table, in sections. The format thus allows for the depiction of a continuous narrative or journey.

The traditional alternative format in East Asian painting is the vertical hanging scroll, which is rarely as long.

Rhodium(III) chloride refers to inorganic compounds with the formula RhCl3(H2O)n, where n varies from 0 to 3. These are diamagnetic solids featuring octahedral Rh(III) centres. Depending on the value of n, the material is either a dense brown solid or a soluble reddish salt. The soluble trihydrated (n = 3) salt is widely used to prepare compounds used in homogeneous catalysis, notably for the industrial production of acetic acid and hydroformylation.

Matt Johnson (born 15 August 1961) is an English singer-songwriter best known as the vocalist and only constant member of his band The The. He is also a film soundtrack composer (Cineola), publisher (Fifty First State Press), broadcaster (Radio Cineola), and conservationist/local activist.
Christian Albert, Burgrave and Count of Dohna (1621–1677) was a general in the army of Brandenburg. He also served as privy councillor and governor of Küstrin.

Michael Korovkin (born December 28, 1948 in Russia) is a socio-cultural anthropologist, novelist and poet. He began as a neuro-physiologist in Russia and after emigrating in 1976 he went on to study anthropology in Canada, England and the Netherlands. His academic work focuses on medical anthropology and European and Mediterranean studies. His literary work covers a diverse range of socio-cultural milieus and psychological perspectives, extending cultured and incisive insights into the human condition. He has lived in Italy since 1989.

Salah Shahrour (Lang-ar; born 1 February 1988 in Aleppo, Syria) is a Syrian footballer who plays as a defender for Al-Nejmeh, which competes in the Lebanese Premier League the top division level in Lebanon and is currently a member of the Syria national football team.

Luise Kähler (née Girnth; 12 January 1869 – 22 September 1955) was a Prussian-German trade unionist and politician. Born in Berlin, she worked as a domestic servant and seamstress before becoming politically active with the Social Democratic Party of Germany in 1902. In 1906, she co-founded a union for women in domestic trades in Hamburg, later becoming president of the Union of Domestic Workers of Germany in 1913. Kähler was a member of the Prussian Landtag from 1923 to 1933. After World War II, she became a founding member of the Socialist Unity Party of Germany.

Ernie Camacho (born February 1, 1956) is an American former professional baseball pitcher. He played in MLB for the Oakland Athletics (1980), Pittsburgh Pirates (1981), Cleveland Indians (1983–1987), Houston Astros (1988), San Francisco Giants (1989–1990), and St. Louis Cardinals (1990).
**Martin Schütz** (1963–2018) was a Swiss theoretical chemist and university professor.

*Mélite* is a comedy play by Pierre Corneille, first performed in 1629. The plot revolves around misunderstandings caused by forged love letters. Éraste loves Mélite, but she falls for his friend Tircis. Éraste's scheme to make Tircis believe Mélite loves another man backfires, leading to despair and madness. Eventually, Éraste confesses, Tircis and Mélite marry, and Éraste marries Cloris.

Charles William Hargens, Jr. (1893−1997) was an American painter. He created over 3000 covers for magazines such as The Saturday Evening Post, Collier's, Country Gentleman, Farm Journal, Boys' Life, The Open Road for Boys, along with advertisements for companies such as Coca-Cola and covers for over 300 books, including the Zane Grey Western novels of the 1930s and 1940s.

He worked for over 80 years and died on January 30, 1997, at 103 years of age. He is buried at Glenside Cemetery in Montgomery County Pennsylvania.
GE Power (formerly known as GE Energy) is an American energy technology company, owned by General Electric.

*Fear* is the third studio album by Toad the Wet Sprocket, released on August 27, 1991, by Columbia Records. The album was recorded at Granny's House in Reno, Nevada, and produced, recorded, and mixed by Gavin MacKillop, who would also produce their next two albums.

Columbia Records financed the mixing of fourteen songs, twelve of which were chosen for release on the album. The album spawned the singles "Is It for Me", "All I Want", "Hold Her Down", "Walk on the Ocean", and "I Will Not Take These Things for Granted".

*Monarchs, Rulers, Dynasties and Kingdoms of the World* is a reference book that begins with a bibliography and a 150-page "Guide to Dynasties and States." The guide provides brief descriptions of various dynasties and states, indicating where related lists can be found within the book. It serves as a reference for identifying rulers in different parts of the world throughout history. The book also outlines some royal family connections and attempts to locate early realms on a modern map, providing historical context for each country or province.

Natalia Maliszewska (born September 16, 1995) is a Polish short track speed skater. She won a silver medal at the 2018 World Championships and a gold medal at the 2019 European Championships, both in the 500m event.

Hadia Tajik (born 18 July 1983) is a Pakistani-Norwegian jurist, journalist and politician from the Labour Party. She served as Minister of Labour and Social Inclusion from 2021 to 2022. She previously served as Minister of Culture from 2012 to 2013. She was 29 years of age at the time and became the youngest minister to serve in the Norwegian government. She is the first Cabinet member that is a Muslim. Tajik has served as a Member of Parliament representing Rogaland since 2017, and Oslo from 2009 to 2017. She was also the party's deputy leader from 2015 until 2022.
Manilal Mohandas Gandhi (28 October 1892 – 5 April 1956) was the second son of Mohandas Gandhi and Kasturba Gandhi.

Stuyvesant Street is an east–west street in New York City's borough of Manhattan, which originally ran through Petrus Stuyvesant's farm to his manor house. Today, it runs between Third and Fourth Avenues in the East Village. The street deviates from Manhattan's grid plan, and is part of the St. Mark's Historic District. The Hamilton Fish House is located on the street. A portion of the street east of Second Avenue is now Abe Lebewohl Park, and another section between Third and Fourth Avenues is occupied by a Cooper Union building.
Casey's theorem is a theorem in Euclidean geometry that is a generalization of Ptolemy's theorem. It relates to four circles tangent to a common circle.

Manny Pacquiao vs. Jeff Horn was a boxing match for the WBO welterweight title held on July 2, 2017, at Suncorp Stadium in Brisbane, Australia. Horn won by controversial unanimous decision, sparking criticism from analysts and fans.
**Christina Elliot** CB, CBE (born 1963/1964) is a retired Royal Air Force air vice-marshal and controller of the RAF Benevolent Fund.

A Beautifully Foolish Endeavor is a science fiction novel by American author Hank Green that was published on July 7, 2020. Green's second novel, also starring main character April May, it is the sequel to An Absolutely Remarkable Thing and the final installment in the Carl Saga.

Paleontology in New Hampshire refers to paleontological research occurring within or conducted by people from the U.S. state of New Hampshire. Fossils are very rare in New Hampshire because so much of the state's geology is highly metamorphic. The state's complicated geologic history has made dating its rocks and the fossils they contain "a difficult task." The state's Devonian rocks are especially metamorphosed, yet its Mississippian rocks formed too recently to have been subject to the same metamorphism. Nevertheless, despite the geologic complications some fossils have been discovered in the state.

During the early Paleozoic, New Hampshire was covered by a warm, shallow sea that would come to be inhabited by creatures like brachiopods, bryozoans, corals, crinoids, and trilobites. Geologic forces raised the local elevation during the Devonian, and local sediments were eroded away from the state rather than deposited, leading to a gap in the local rock record. Erosion continued through the Mesozoic and the early to mid Cenozoic, leaving few rocks of that age, and no fossils. By the Ice Age, the state was being worked over by glaciers and had similar wildlife to the modern Canadian Arctic.

New Hampshire fossils had attracted scientific attention by the late 1800s when C. H. Hitchcock reported local invertebrate remains preserved near Littleton. Other notable finds include fossils preserved in metamorphic rock that are still recognizable despite the extreme geologic forces exerted on these rocks.
Ya'akov Ekhoiz (Lang-he; born 29 May 1957) is an Israeli former footballer.
Adil Rza bey oglu Isgandarov (Lang-az; 5 May 1910 – 18 September 1978) was a Soviet and Azerbaijani director, stage and film actor and pedagogue. People's Artist of the USSR (1959).

Dissymmetry of lift: 2–20 (also known as asymmetry of lift: 342 or asymmetric lift) in rotorcraft aerodynamics refers to an unequal amount of lift on opposite sides of the rotor disc. It is a phenomenon that affects single-rotor helicopters and autogyros in forward flight.

A rotor blade that is moving in the same direction as the aircraft is called the advancing blade and the blade moving in the opposite direction is called the retreating blade. When viewed from above, most American helicopter rotors turn counter-clockwise; French and Russian helicopters turn clockwise.: 141

Balancing lift across the rotor disc is important to a helicopter's stability. The amount of lift generated by an airfoil is proportional to the square of its airspeed (velocity). In a hover, the rotor blades have equal airspeeds and therefore equal lift. However, in forward flight the advancing blade has a higher airspeed than the retreating blade, creating uneven lift across the rotor disc.: 1–104

John James Hughes (1814 – 17 June 1889) was a Welsh engineer, businessman and founder of the city of Donetsk. The village was originally named Yuzovka (Hughesovka, Lang-ru) after Hughes, ("Yuz" being a Russian approximation of Hughes), formally becoming a town in May 1917 and later, in 1924, being renamed to Stalino (Lang-uk); in 1961, the name was changed to Donetsk.
Acacia tingoorensis, also known as Tingoora wattle, is a tree belonging to the genus Acacia and the subgenus Juliflorae that is native to eastern Australia.
Nina is a French television series. It has run since 2015 on France 2.

Edward A. Keenan (October 15, 1894 – April 12, 1970) was an American politician who served as the 34th Mayor of Burlington, Vermont. Keenan was the last Republican elected to the mayoralty of Burlington until Peter C. Brownell was thirty years later.

Silent Sejm (Lang-pl; Lang-lt), also known as the Mute Sejm, is the name given to the session of the Sejm parliament of the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth of 1 February 1717 held in Warsaw. A civil war in the Commonwealth was used by the Russian Tsar Peter the Great as an opportunity to intervene as a mediator. It marked the end of Augustus II of Poland's attempts to create an absolute monarchy in Poland, and the beginning of the Russian Empire's increasing influence and control over the Commonwealth.

Omsk hemorrhagic fever is a viral hemorrhagic fever caused by a Flavivirus.

It was found in Siberia and was named for an outbreak in the city of Omsk. First records of the new virus appeared around 1940–1943.

Boomplay is a streaming service developed and owned by Transsnet Music Limited. Launched in Nigeria in 2015, it focuses on African and urban music content. As of 2024, Boomplay has over 70 million monthly active users.

Boomplay has licensing deals with Universal Music Group, Warner Music Group, Sony Music Group, and Believe Digital. In 2017, it won the ‘Best African App’ Award at the AppsAfrica Innovation Awards. In October 2021, Billboard announced that Boomplay streams would be added to the data used in collating the Billboard Hot 100 and Billboard 200 charts.

Laura Natalia Esquivel (born May 18, 1994), also known as Laura Esquivel, is an Argentine actress and singer. She began her career on *Codigo F.A.M.A. Argentina* and rose to prominence as the lead in the telenovela *Patito Feo*, which achieved international success.

A composite film is a film composed of shorter, autonomous stories that are interrelated within a coherent whole. Unifying elements such as recurring characters, shared incidents, common settings, or similar themes are often used to connect the individual stories. The film score can also link stories through a unified score, composite score, or theme and variation.

HNoMS Rauma was an Otra-class minesweeper built in 1939 for the Royal Norwegian Navy. Captured by the Germans during the 1940 invasion of Norway and renamed Kamerun, she was returned to the Norwegians after the end of the Second World War and recommissioned in 1947. Rauma remained in service until being sold for scrapping in 1963.

The 22nd Seanad Éireann first met on September 12, 2002. It comprised 43 senators elected from vocational panels, 6 from universities, and 11 nominated by the Taoiseach. During its term, one senator was replaced after death, and four Taoiseach nominees were replaced after being elected to the Dáil. Ten elected senators also won seats in the Dáil, but their seats remained vacant.

JD Eicher (born July 27, 1986) is an American singer, songwriter, producer, and novelist from the Youngstown, Ohio area.

Both billed as JD Eicher & the Goodnights and independently as JD Eicher, Eicher has released nine albums—one of which also featured a Deluxe Edition—as well as a live EP and vinyl collection of his most popular songs.

Eicher also created an EP of his music to accompany the release of Nicholas Sparks' 20th novel called Two by Two. The musician and the author toured the country to promote this project.

Touring mostly in the United States, Eicher has a strong following in many regions of the nation. Eicher also occasionally tours in Europe and has fans in many cities abroad. Additionally, a number of Eicher's songs receive radio airplay and have been used in businesses, television ad campaigns, and television shows.

Yawalapití is an indigenous language spoken in the Xingu Indigenous Park of Brazil. In 2006, it was reported to have only 8 native speakers. It belongs to the Arawakan language family, within the Paresí–Xingu subgroup. Yawalapití shares a similar phonemic inventory with Waurá.

Michael Kevin O'Loughlin (born 20 February 1977) is a former professional Australian rules footballer, who played his entire Australian Football League career with the Sydney Swans.

O'Loughlin was named a member of the Indigenous Team of the Century. He was the third player with Indigenous heritage to play 300 AFL games. He twice achieved All-Australian selection, played for Australia twice in the International Rules Series, and was a Fos Williams Medallist as best player for South Australia in State of Origin. O'Loughlin was the first Sydney Swans player to play more than 300 career games. In 303 games he kicked 521 career goals.

The Hiller YH-32 Hornet was an early attempt to create a jet-powered helicopter using ramjets at the rotor tips. This design eliminated the need for a tail rotor, but suffered from high fuel consumption, poor range, and excessive noise due to the inefficiency of ramjets at subsonic rotor tip speeds.

Evaluated by the United States Army as the YH-32 and the United States Navy as the XHOE-1, two YH-32s were modified in 1957 for trials as armed helicopters. While these tests proved the viability of helicopters as weapons platforms, the Hornet's marginal performance led to the program's cancellation.

Miniclip SA is a mobile game publisher and former browser game website that launched in 2001. It was started by Robert Small and Tihan Presbie with a budget of £40,000. In 2008, the company was valued at over £275 million.

In 2009, Miniclip hosted over 400 applications on its website.

In 2015, Tencent acquired majority stakes of Miniclip. In December 2016, Miniclip crossed 1 billion downloads across its mobile games on iOS, Android, and Windows devices. In March 2022, Miniclip announced that it had reached 4 billion downloads, with 8 Ball Pool alone accounting for 1 billion.

In April 2022, Miniclip announced that it would begin prioritizing its mobile games. As a result, the browser game portal was shut down in July 2022 and the website lost all but its two most popular games, Agar.io and 8 Ball Pool.

In June 2022, Miniclip acquired SYBO, the publisher of Subway Surfers, in an undisclosed deal.
Bing Yeh is a Taiwanese-American businessman. He co-founded Silicon Storage Technology (SST) in 1989 and Greenliant Systems in 2010.

Pelican Waters is a suburb of Caloundra in the Sunshine Coast Region, Queensland, Australia. In the 2016 census, Pelican Waters had a population of 6,277 people.

Interstate 285 (I-285) in the US state of North Carolina is the designation for a freeway connecting the cities of Lexington and Winston-Salem. The route was approved in February 2018 and was first signed in November 2018. An auxiliary route of I-85, it branches off of its parent route and runs northeast, bypassing central Lexington, before turning due north and heading toward Winston-Salem, terminating at an interchange with I-40. I-285 is cosigned with US Highway 52 (US 52) for its entire route, and as of November 2018, still uses US 52 milemarkers and exit numbers. Other routes cosigned along parts of the route include I-85 Business (I-85 Bus), US 29, US 70, and North Carolina Highway 8 (NC 8). I-285 has been approved for an extension along the US 52 freeway through Winston-Salem to meet the future Winston-Salem Northern Beltway.
Luise Koch (11 October 1860 Bremen – 14 March 1934 in Bremen) was a German educator, politician (DDP) and women's rights activist.
Callulops fojaensis is a species of frog in the family Microhylidae. It is only known from the Foja mountains in West Papua (Indonesia).

Unconscious communication encompasses the exchange of information without conscious awareness, operating on both intrapersonal and interpersonal levels. The unconscious mind, believed to house biological instincts, influences thoughts, feelings, and behaviors outside of conscious perception. Sigmund Freud popularized the concept of the unconscious, suggesting that repressed emotions are projected onto others.

Intrapersonal unconscious communication involves the impact of dreams, past experiences, or hypnosis on a person's choices and experiences without their conscious knowledge. Some theories suggest that intrapersonal communication evolved to avoid the discomfort of silence.

Interpersonal unconscious communication manifests through unintentional nonverbal cues such as facial expressions, body language, tone of voice, and speech patterns. These cues are interpreted by others, influencing their understanding and reactions. Studies indicate that individuals instinctively mimic emotional facial expressions, demonstrating an unconscious connection in interpersonal communication.

Kris Fisher is a fictional character from the British Channel 4 soap opera Hollyoaks, played by Gerard McCarthy. The character first appeared on-screen in September 2006 and left in August 2010 after McCarthy quit the show. He is noted for his storylines involving the PEP drug, a bisexual love triangle, his outlandish personality and being the only permanent cross dressing character in a British soap opera. Following his departure, it was announced that McCarthy would return in November 2010 for a short stint.
Eric Sharp, Baron Sharp of Grimsdyke, CBE (1916–1994) was a British businessman who served as chairman of Cable & Wireless plc from 1980 to 1990.

Down High School is a co-educational grammar school located in Downpatrick, County Down, Northern Ireland. Established in 1933, the school educates students aged 11 to 18 and has an enrollment of approximately 1,080 pupils. The school's motto is "Floreat Dunum, Absque dole Labore Nihil," which translates to "May Down flourish, Nothing achieved without effort."

The school's campus incorporates elements of the former county gaol, including the gate house and lowered stone walls. The school crest is based on the badge of John De Courcy, who captured Downpatrick in 1177. The current principal is Mrs. M. Perry.

Kenneth Francis (born December 1, 1969) is an American NASCAR crew chief. He began his racing career at the age of eight, racing go-karts. He continued to drive the race car until 1996 when he began working on engines for Butch Mock Motorsports. Six years later, Francis became a crew chief for the first time while he was employed at Robert Yates Racing. He is currently employed at Hendrick Motorsports as the Vehicle Technical Director organization-wide.

Rhodium(III) chloride is the inorganic compound with the formula RhCl3. It is a dark red solid that is insoluble in water. RhCl3(H2O)3 is used as a precursor to prepare a variety of complexes, some of which are used in homogeneous catalysis. Beginning in the 1960s, it was found to catalyze reactions involving carbon monoxide, hydrogen, and alkenes.

The Federal Network Agency (Lang-de or BNetzA) is the German regulatory office for electricity, gas, telecommunications, post and railway markets. It is a federal agency of the Federal Ministry for Economic Affairs and Climate Action and headquartered in Bonn, Germany.

Dessie Glynn (7 June 1928 – 6 January 2017) was a Republic of Ireland international footballer who played for Drumcondra and Shelbourne in the late 1940s and 1950s, scoring 111 goals in his League of Ireland career. Glynn was also Drumcondra's all-time top goalscorer with 96 goals between 1949 and 1956. Eamon Dunphy described Glynn as "a splendidly versatile centre-forward, a scorer and maker of goals". Glynn grew up in Drumcondra, Dublin, was educated at St. Vincent's C.B.S. and worked for the Irish civil service. In 1958 he spent nine months in hospital, suffering from tuberculosis – a condition which effectively ended his playing career. He later coached in New York.

Martyrdom in Judaism is one of the main examples of Jews doing a kiddush Hashem, a Hebrew term which means "sanctification of [the] name". An example of this is public self-sacrifice in accordance with Jewish practice and identity, with the possibility of being killed for no other reason than being Jewish. There are specific conditions in Jewish law that deal with the details of self-sacrifice, be it willing or unwilling.

The opposite or converse of kiddush Hashem is chillul Hashem ("Desecration [of] God's Name" in Hebrew) and Jews are obligated to avoid it according to Halakha (Jewish religious law). There are instances, such as when they are faced with forced conversion to another religion, when Jews should choose martyrdom and sacrifice their lives rather than commit a chillul Hashem which desecrates the honor of God. Martyrdom in Judaism is thus driven by both the desire to Sanctify God's Name concurrently and the wish to avoid the Desecration of God's Name.

In Hebrew a martyr is known as a kaddosh which means "[a] holy [one]", and martyrs are known as kedoshim meaning "holy [ones]". Thus the six million Jews who were murdered in the Holocaust are known as the Kedoshim.

Jewish history is replete with many episodes in which Jews who lived in different times and places chose to become individual and mass martyrs.

*Lygodium microphyllum*, also known as Old World climbing fern, is a fern species native to tropical Africa, Asia, Australia, and the Pacific. It has become naturalized in the Caribbean and the southeastern United States, where it is considered an invasive species. It damages wetland ecosystems, reduces native plant diversity, and poses fire risks. Insects and mites are used for containment. Fibers are used to weave traditional hats in the Philippines.
F.C. Netanya (Lang-he) is an Israeli football club based in Netanya. The club currently plays in Liga Gimel Sharon division.

*Calamus* is a genus of climbing palms in the family Arecaceae. These dioecious, leaf-climbing lianas use hooks and spines to scramble, with stems growing up to 200 meters long. The stems are harvested for canes and furniture. Some species, like *Calamus draco*, produce "Dragon's blood" resin.

Panhard was a French manufacturer of automobiles, railbuses, and military vehicles. Originally called Panhard et Levassor, it was founded in 1887 by René Panhard and Émile Levassor. The company sold its first automobile in 1890, based on a Daimler engine license. After World War II, the company was renamed Panhard. The last Panhard passenger car was built in 1967. In 2012, Renault Trucks Defense acquired Panhard.

Michael Rowan O'Driscoll (born 8 October 1978) is an Irish former rugby union player. He played for Munster in the Pro12 and Heineken Cup, and played internationally for Ireland. He was an integral senior player for Munster and captained the province numerous times, particularly during the 2008–09 season.

"Flash" is a song by Japanese girl group Perfume, released digitally on March 16, 2016, as a single from their album *Cosmic Explorer*. The song incorporates J-pop and EDM genres and was produced by Yasutaka Nakata. The choreography blends martial arts and Tai chi. Its music video, directed by Yusuke Tanaka, has garnered over 60 million views on YouTube.

The 2014 Vodacom Cup was played between 7 March and 16 May 2014 and was the 17th edition of this annual domestic cup competition. This edition of the Vodacom Cup was played between fourteen provincial rugby union teams in South Africa from the Currie Cup Premier and First Divisions, as well as the Limpopo Blue Bulls and Simba XV from Kenya.

When Hope Calls is an American drama television series. The series is a spin-off of Hallmark Channel's When Calls the Heart, which is based on the Canadian West book series by Janette Oke. It premiered on August 30, 2019 as the inaugural original programming on Hallmark Movies Now, the Hallmark Channel's digital streaming service, with new episodes being released through October 25, 2019. The series was subsequently aired on Hallmark Channel in early 2020. The series was renewed for a second season and premiered on Great American Family on December 18, 2021.

Manny Pacquiao vs. Jeff Horn, billed as the Battle of Brisbane, was a boxing match for the WBO welterweight championship. The event took place on July 2, 2017 at Suncorp Stadium in Brisbane, Australia. Horn won the fight by unanimous decision and claimed his first world title in boxing.

Rifle bedding is a process of providing a rigid and consistent foundation for a rifle’s operational components, by creating a stable and close-fitting bearing surface between the gun’s functional parts (i.e. the receiver housing the barrelled action) and its support part (i.e. the stock) that do not deform with pressure or shift under the shear stress of the recoil from firing. This is for the goal of accurizing the rifle and (to a lesser extent) prolonging the life of the stock. The bedding process is often an aftermarket improvement.

The team rosters for the 2001 South American U-20 Championship football tournament held in Ecuador. The ten national teams involved in the tournament were required to register a squad of 20 players; only players in these squads were eligible to take part in the tournament.

Players name marked in bold have been capped at full international level.

Powers Auditorium, formerly known as the Warner Theatre, is a Youngstown, Ohio, performing arts venue. Designed by Rapp and Rapp, it opened on May 14, 1931, as part of the Warner Brothers film company's theater chain, built as a memorial to Sam Warner.

The theater operated until 1968, when it was slated for demolition. Following public outcry and a donation from the Edward W. Powers family, it was renovated in 1969 and renamed Powers Auditorium. The auditorium underwent acoustical renovation to become a concert hall, and in September 1969, the Youngstown Symphony performed there. Powers Auditorium was added to the National Register of Historic Places in 1980.

Difunta Correa (died c. 1840s) is a folk saint venerated in Argentina. According to legend, Deolinda Correa died of thirst in the San Juan desert while trying to reach her sick husband, a conscript in the Argentine civil wars. Her body was discovered by gauchos, who found her baby alive, still nursing from her breast.

Her gravesite in Vallecito has become a major shrine, where devotees, including cattle herders, truck drivers, and gauchos, leave offerings, particularly bottles of water. Though not recognized by the Catholic Church, she is believed to perform miracles and intercede for the living.

The Wilson's snipe (Gallinago delicata) is a small, stocky shorebird with a long bill and mottled brown plumage. They are in the family Scolopacidae. Adults are 23–28 cm in length with a 39–45 cm wingspan. They breed in marshes, bogs, tundra, and wet meadows in Canada, the northern United States, and Russia. Some populations are year-round residents, while others migrate to the southern United States, the Caribbean, and northern South America. They forage in soft mud for insects, earthworms, and plant material. Males perform a "winnowing" display during courtship. While the species is not currently threatened, local populations are sensitive to wetland draining.

Advance America (formerly Citizens Concerned for the Constitution) is a conservative political advocacy group in the U.S. state of Indiana. Advance America claims that it is a "non-partisan tax exempt, educational organization." It claims affiliation with approximately 4,000 Indiana churches, nearly one third of all churches in the state of Indiana.
Jasaw Chan Kʼawiil I also known as Ruler A, Ah Cacao and Sky Rain, (before 682-734), was an ajaw of the Maya city of Tikal. He took the throne on May 3, 682 and reigned until his death.

Alexander Tarasov-Rodionov (1885–1938) was a Russian/Soviet writer. He joined the Bolshevik party in 1905 and participated in the 1917 Revolution and the Russian Civil War. His novel *Chocolate*, published in 1922, was reprinted several times and translated into other languages. He was arrested and executed in 1938, and rehabilitated in 1956.

Sir Frederick Wollaston Mann KCMG (2 May 1869 – 29 May 1958) was the chief justice of the Australian state of Victoria between 1 October 1935 and 31 January 1944. He was also Victoria’s lieutenant governor between 12 May 1936 and May 1945. Mann was nicknamed the “Little Gentleman” because of his height (he was 168 cm tall) and he was unfailingly courteous. He had a reputation of being a careful judge delivering decisions of precision and clarity. In 1935, he became the first Australian born person to become chief justice of Victoria.
Kylie Nicole Sicangco Padilla (IPA-tl; born 25 January 1993) is a Filipino actress, singer and model. She is the daughter of actor Robin Padilla and Liezl Sicangco.

Oreal Donald Perras (1942 – 2017) was a Canadian professional wrestler, best known as Ivan Koloff. Billed as being from Russia, he defeated Bruno Sammartino in 1971 to win the WWWF World Heavyweight Championship. He died of liver cancer.

*The Harold Peary Show* is a situation comedy that aired on CBS from September 17, 1950, to June 13, 1951, starring Harold Peary. The show failed to gain popularity, and was very similar to Peary's previous show, *The Great Gildersleeve*.

World Wide Adventures is a generalized name applied to Warner Bros. live-action short films of the 1960s. Usually, the trade magazines like BoxOffice only listed the one-reelers (running about 10 minutes in length) under this heading, with the longer films simply dubbed “specials.” For the most part, this was a handy marketing logo for a wide range of shorts of the documentary genre.

The Committee of the Verkhovna Rada of Ukraine on Environmental Policy, Nature Resources Utilization and Elimination of the Consequences of Chornobyl Catastrophe (Lang-uk, Komitet Verkhovnoi Rady Ukrainy z pytan’ ekolohichnoi polityky, pryrodokorystuvannia ta likvidatsii naslidkiv Chornobyl’s’koi katastrofy) is a standing committee of the Verkhovna Rada, Ukraine's unicameral parliament. The committee consists of 15 people's deputies in the parliament's 8th convocation. Its entire composition was approved on December 4, 2014.

Valmiera is a state city in Latvia, located on the Gauja River. With a population of 22,376 as of 2024, it serves as the center of Valmiera Municipality. First mentioned as a town in 1323, Valmiera was a member of the Hanseatic League from the 14th–16th centuries, fostering trade and development. The city has a rich history, experiencing periods of devastation and growth, and has been an important industrial, educational, and sports center in Latvia.

A diatreme, sometimes known as a maar-diatreme volcano, is a volcanic pipe formed by a gaseous explosion. When magma rises up through a crack in Earth's crust and makes contact with a shallow body of groundwater, rapid expansion of heated water vapor and volcanic gases can cause a series of explosions. A relatively shallow crater (known as a maar) is left, and a rock-filled fracture (the actual diatreme) in the crust. Diatremes breach the surface and produce a steep, inverted cone shape.

The term diatreme has been applied more generally to any concave body of broken rock formed by explosive or hydrostatic forces, whether or not it is related to volcanism. The word comes from Ancient Greek δία- (dia-) 'through, across, over' and τρῆμα (trêma) 'hole, aperture'.
Uptown New York is a 1932 American Pre-Code film directed by Victor Schertzinger and starring Jack Oakie, Shirley Grey, and Leon Ames. It is based on the story by Vina Delmar.

Terry Mutchler is an American attorney and writer. She was formerly a journalist and public servant in Pennsylvania. As a journalist, she won several awards for her reporting and was Associated Press' Bureau Chief in New Jersey and Alaska. She later became an attorney, and was appointed the first executive director of the newly established Pennsylvania Office of Open Records. Her memoir, Under This Beautiful Dome: A Senator, A Journalist, and the Politics of Gay Love in America, was a finalist for the Lambda Literary Award for Lesbian Memoir or Biography.
